name: adam bien
bio: Developer (Architect), Consultant, Trainer (https://airhacks.live), Podcaster (https://airhacks.fm), AWS Hero and Java (Champion) enthusiast Adam Bien (https://adambien.blog) has been using Java since JDK 1.0 and JavaScript since LiveScript and still enjoys writing code. Adam regularly organises Java / Web / Cloud / Architectures online live workshops https://airhacks.live and a monthly Q&A live streaming show: https://airhacks.tv.

name: Adam Smolnik
bio:
Adam Smolnik is a Cloud Architect at HOSTERSI in Rybnik (Poland), a certified AWS Advanced Consulting Partner and AWS Channel Partner, specializing in implementing solutions offered by Amazon Web Services, server management, DevOps services, cybersecurity and performance optimization. Prior to HOSTERSI, Adam worked as a cloud & solutions architect, application developer, consultant and designer for companies like EPAM, Pitney Bowes, IBM and Kroll Ontrack. He also holds the title Amazon AWS Authorized Instructor Champion and conducts Amazon AWS courses at CloudTeam - Cloud Computing Experts in Poland.


Adam is also a co-founder of the AWS User Group Poland, an active speaker at Cloud conferences, instructor at Cloud and Software workshops as well as co-organizer of the Cloudyna conference - the best Cloud conference in Poland.

name: Agustinus Nalwan
bio: Living to innovate to make people’s lives easier and with over 30 years of experience in software development, Gus has worked in various industries from 3D/Animation, Games Development, Desktop Software, Native Apps, Computer Vision, AI, and Generative AI

name: Ahmed Bebars
bio: I am deeply passionate about technology and highly motivated to help people be more productive, at a faster pace. My focus is on leading teams in delivering resilient infrastructure and building platforms that improve enterprise productivity. I have a strong belief in the power of scalability and bring this sense of purpose to my mission in creating platforms that help shape everyone’s experience for the better. In my role as Principal Engineer on the Developer Platforms mission at The New York Times, I lead the implementation and delivery of robust and scalable Kubernetes-based solutions. I leverage deep technical leadership experience and engineering expertise to architect and deliver a scalable infrastructure platform and enhanced user experience to meet the organization’s business goals. As a leader, I build collaborative teams, which help to break down siloed efforts that duplicate work, impede innovation, and create chokepoints in knowledge sharing. I strive to strike the right balance of removing the friction of obstacles, while championing clear standards of excellence, so that people have the ability to use a platform and build on top it, ultimately making them more productive.

name: Ahmed Samir
bio: AWS Community Hero and Solution Architect at Alinma Bank with 17+ years of experience in IT and digital transformation. Ahmed specializes in cloud-native architecture for financial services, driving digital banking initiatives . I am leading three Arabic-language meetups in Riyadh — AWS, Amazon SageMaker, and Kubernetes — and has organized 45+ community events for AWS to grow and empower the cloud community across the MENA region. I am AWS and Kubernetes certified and passionate about making cloud knowledge accessible in Arabic.

name: Aidan Steele
bio: AWS Hero based in Melbourne, Australia. I've been using AWS in some form or another since 2008, and full time for the last 5-10 years. My interests are security, containers and serverless - in no particular order.

name: AJ Stuyvenberg
bio:
AJ Stuyvenberg is a Staff Engineer at Datadog, and has been a member of the Serverless community since early 2017. His work focuses on Serverless and Distributed Systems observability.


AJ is an open source author and maintains several projects which improve the Serverless developer experience. Hes spoken at multiple conferences including AWS re:Invent and AWS Summits, and frequently writes about Serverless topics on his blog. Previously, he was Principal Engineer at Serverless Inc  the company behind the Serverless Framework.


In his spare time, AJ is an avid BASE jumper, and enjoys flying his wingsuit in the Alps.

name: Albert Suwandhi
bio: Albert Suwandhi is an academic and a Cloud Infrastructure Engineer from Medan, Indonesia. He is an IT Lecturer at Universitas IBBI and Universitas Pelita Harapan where he promotes and lectures cloud computing related courses using AWS.

name: Aleksandar Simovic
bio:
Aleksandar Simovic is a Senior Software Engineer at Science Exchange and co-author of Serverless Applications with Node.js with Slobodan Stojanovic, published by Manning Publications. Additionally, he writes on Medium on both business and technical aspects of serverless. He's also a Wardley Mapper.


Aleksandar is a Claudia.js core team member, and is also involved with other serverless related open-source projects such as Claudia-Bot-Builder, Scotty.js, and Desole.io. He has published over a dozen open-source serverless applications to the AWS Serverless Application Repository. One of his latest serverless experiments is a Serverless JARVIS, an Alexa skill that can create serverless applications using voice commands. See Aleksandar's serverless apps in the Repository

name: Alex DeBrie
bio: Previously, Alex worked as an engineer at Stedi, Serverless, Inc., and Hudl. If you go even further back, Alex was briefly a corporate lawyer. He lives in Omaha, NE with his wife and four kids.

name: Alex Lau
bio: Alex Lau is a Lead Instructor of Tecky Academy with a focus on Full Stack, Mobile App, and AWS technologies. Enthusiastic about teaching and sharing, Alex has been an active leader in the Hong Kong developer community since 2015.

name: Allen Helton
bio: I’m an Ecosystem Engineer at Momento with a background in building scalable, event-driven systems. These days, I spend a lot of time exploring how AI agents can actually work at enterprise scale. I focus on challenges like determinism, orchestration, and fault tolerance. I enjoy sharing what I learn. I write, livestream, and host podcasts to help developers understand complex systems in a way that feels approachable and practical. Community is at the core of everything I do. From the Believe in Serverless community to conference talks and one-on-one mentoring, I try to make space where people can learn and build together. I believe good humans build good technology, and I do my best to live that out in everything I do. Happy coding!

name: Alvaro Hernandez
bio:
Alvaro is a passionate database and software developer. He founded and works as the CEO of OnGres, a PostGres startup set to disrupt the database market. He has been dedicated to PostgreSQL and R&D in databases for two decades. R&D that includes crazy projects like spending $40K on AWS just to benchmark and compare PostgreSQL and MongoDB.


An open source advocate and developer at heart, Alvaro is a well-known member of the PostgreSQL Community, to which he has contributed founding the non-profit Fundacion PostgreSQL and the Spanish PostgreSQL User Group.


You can find him frequently speaking at PostgreSQL, database, cloud, and Java conferences. He regularly visits and speaks at AWS local meetups and re:Invent. Every year, Alvaro travels approximately three-four times around the globein 2020, he will hit the milestone of having delivered 100 tech talks.

name: Amy Tseng
bio: I love building modern data and AI solutions that actually deliver value. Over the years—in the financial industry and across large, complex organizations—I’ve led major data modernization programs, rethought enterprise architecture, and built platforms that scale. Whether it’s driving cloud transformation, shaping data strategy, or coaching teams, I bring curiosity, clarity, and energy to the work. I’m passionate about using data and AI to unlock new possibilities and help organizations move faster, smarter, and with more confidence.

name: Anahit Pogosova
bio: Anahit is a Cloud Architecture & Engineering Lead at F-Secure, where she leads the Cloud Center of Excellence: a team focused on AWS governance, security, and cloud best practices. With over 16 years of experience across full-stack and data engineering, her career has evolved from building monolithic on-premises systems to architecting modern, resilient, and scalable cloud solutions. Anahit is also an active voice in the tech community. She speaks at conferences and meetups, appears on podcasts and YouTube channels, and teaches online courses.

name: Ananda Dwi Rahmawati
bio: AWS Container Hero

name: Anders Bjørnestad [AWS Hero]
bio: AWS Community Hero [Oslo, Norway]

name: Angela
bio:
Angela is Data Platform Manager at Trustpilot. Passionate about knowledge sharing, coaching, and public speaking, she is committed to leading by example and empowering people to seek and develop the skills they need to achieve their goals.


Angela has a proven history of transitioning legacy applications to new platforms and product offerings. She is driven to build scalable solutions with the latest technologies while migrating away from monolithic solutions using serverless applications and event-driven architecture. She is a co-organizer of the Copenhagen AWS User Group and a frequent speaker about serverless technologies at AWS Summits, AWS Community Days, ServerlessDays, and more.

name: Anh Ho Viet
bio: I’m the Founder of the AWS Vietnam User Group and the Co-founder & CEO of OSAM, an AWS Consulting Partner in Vietnam. I’m also an AWS Certified Solutions Architect and a passionate advocate for the cloud. At OSAM, I lead an enthusiastic team that has helped many businesses — from SMEs to large enterprises — migrate to the cloud with AWS. We provide a comprehensive range of services, including cloud migration, architecture consulting, solution design on AWS, and Cloud FinOps. My vision for OSAM goes beyond being a cloud service provider; I want us to play a meaningful role in shaping Vietnam’s cloud computing ecosystem. In 2016, I founded the AWS Vietnam User Group to create a space for cloud practitioners to share knowledge and hands-on experience. Since then, the group has grown to over 15,000 members and continues to expand. We host quarterly meetups, connect SMEs with AWS experts, and offer real-time, free consultancy alongside AWS Activate Credits for startups. In 2017, I launched the “Cloud Computing Lectures for Universities” program, where we translate AWS documentation and news into Vietnamese. This initiative equips students with fundamental, up-to-date cloud knowledge and supports their career development. Since then, we’ve helped hundreds of tech students each year build critical cloud skills for the future.

name: Ankit Kumar Gupta
bio:
Ankit is a Solutions Architect at PwC India. He brings deep expertise in Solutions Architecture designing on AWS. Ankit is an AWS user since 2012 and has worked with most AWS services. He is a trusted customer advocate, has multiple AWS Certifications, and has worked on various types of AWS projects. He has completed B.Tech in Computer Science from GIET University, India.


He is an AWS Community leader helping build the AWS Community in India since 2014. He is also a co-organizer of AWS User Group Kolkata, India. Ankit has given multiple sessions on AWS services at various events. He frequently visits Engineering Colleges, providing knowledge sharing sessions on Cloud Technologies, and he also mentors Engineering students.


He keeps himself updated with all the latest services/features on AWS. Currently, he is exploring with AWS AI & ML solutions.

name: Ant Stanley
bio: My first paying tech job was in 1999 doing Y2K upgrades on desktops for a bank in South Africa. Done almost every role in tech, with a career split between London and South Africa. Founder, architect, developer, operator. Early Serverless adopter, now riding the wave of AI powered development.

name: Anton Babenko
bio: Anton is a long-time developer and CTO. He runs a consulting company Betajob in Norway and has helped companies around the globe build solutions using AWS and specializing in Infrastructure-as-Code, DevOps, and reusable infrastructure components since 2015. Through his consulting and ongoing experimentation with new technologies, Anton also created compliance.tf, a product that helps companies in regulated industries simplify and automate compliance when using Terraform. He spends much of his time as an open-source contributor on various Terraform & AWS projects. Such as Terraform AWS modules (provisioned more than 2 billion times), Terraform best practices ebook (https://www.terraform-best-practices.com/), Serverless Terraform framework (https://serverless.tf), Terraform Weekly newsletter (https://weekly.tf), Your Weekly Dose of Terraform live streams (https://www.youtube.com/AntonBabenkoLive), and more (https://github.com/antonbabenko/terraform-aws-devops). Anton enjoys solving current cloud architecture tasks, figuring out generic solutions by getting to the core, and making them available as open-source to benefit the AWS community. Anton co-founded and co-organizes AWS, DevOps, and HashiCorp User Groups in Oslo (Norway) and often speaks at technical meetups and conferences.

name: Anurag Kale
bio: AWS Data Hero. Technical being busy creating a concoction of Analytics, Platforms, Serverless and Software. Some of the things I build break, most work. Iterative approach to work and life.

name: Arseny Zinchneko
bio: DevOps, cloud and infrastructure engineer. Love Linux, OpenSource, and AWS. Tech. blog writer at https://rtfm.co.ua/en

name: Arshad Zackeriya
bio: Arshad Zackeriya (Zack) is a Platform Engineer and an AWS Hero 🦸‍♂️ , based in Wellington, New Zealand 🇳🇿 🥝. As a voice in the AWS cloud community, he serves as the AWS User Group Leader for Wellington, New Zealand. Zack is specializes in AWS Cloud, Linux, and Kubernetes, sharing his expertise as a prolific blogger, podcaster, and mentor. When not architecting cloud solutions, he enjoys swimming, cooking, and collecting Lego Technics

name: Austen Collins
bio: Austen is an entrepreneur and software engineer located in Oakland, CA. His specific focus is on building cheap, scalable Node.js applications while minimizing DevOps requirements as much as possible. An enthusiastic AWS Lambda user from day one, Austen founded the Serverless Framework (formerly JAWS), an open source project and module ecosystem to help everyone build applications exclusively on Lambda, without the hassle and costs required by servers. AWS Lambda can significantly reduce the total cost of ownership of all software projects. Every ambitious developer and company should take advantage of Lambda, and Austen will help you get started.

name: Avi Keinan
bio:
Avi Keinan is a Senior Cloud Engineer at DoIT International, a strategic partner of Amazon Web Services. He specializes in AWS Infrastructure, serverless, and security solutions, enjoys solving complex problems, and is passionate about helping others.


Avi is a member of many online AWS forums and groups where he assists both novice and experienced cloud users with solving complex and simple solutions. He is passionate about the cloud, has a deep understanding of cloud economics, and has a lot of FinOps experience. Avi holds several AWS, Kubernetes, and cloud-related certificates and often speaks at various technical meetups and conferences. He also frequently posts AWS-related videos on his YouTube channel.

name: Awedis Keofteian
bio: Awedis (Avo) is a Senior DevOps Engineer at Washmen and an AWS Community Hero, passionate about building scalable, reliable, and efficient cloud architectures. Outside of work, he enjoys playing basketball, going for a jog, and staying active while exploring new tech ideas.

name: Ayyanar Jeyakrishnan
bio: With 20+ years of IT expertise, I offer strong analytical and technical leadership, specializing in DevOps, Cloud, Big Data, ML, and AI. AWS 15x Certified, AWS UG Bangalore Co-Organizer.

name: Becky Zhang
bio:
Yu Zhang (Becky Zhang) is COO of BootDev, which focuses on Big Data solutions on AWS and high concurrency web architecture. Before she helped run BootDev, she was working at Yubis IT Solutions as an operations manager.


Becky plays a key role in the AWS User Group Shanghai (AWSUGSH), regularly organizing AWS UG events including AWS Tech Meetups and happy hours, gathering AWS talent together to communicate the latest technology and AWS services. As a female in technology industry, Becky is keen on promoting Women in Tech and encourages more women to get involved in the community.


Becky also connects the China AWS User Group with user groups in other regions, including Korea, Japan, and Thailand. She was invited as a panelist at AWS re:Invent 2016 and spoke at the Seoul AWS Summit this April to introduce AWS User Group Shanghai and communicate with other AWS User Groups around the world.


Besides events, Becky also promotes the Shanghai AWS User Group by posting AWS-related tech articles, event forecasts, and event reports to Weibo, Twitter, Meetup.com, and WeChat (which now has over 2000 official account followers).

name: Ben Bridts
bio: Ben is an AWS Technologist at Cloudar and has been using AWS for over ten years. He works with businesses ranging from start-ups to enterprises. He provides architectural and operational support and shares his experiences along the way.

name: Ben Ellerby
bio:
Ellerby is the Founder of aleios and a dedicated member of the Serverless community. He is the editor of Serverless Transformation: a blog, newsletter, and podcast which share tools, techniques, and use cases for all things Serverless.


He co-organizes the Serverless User Group in London, is part of the ServerlessDays London organizing team, and regularly speaks about Serverless around the world.


At aleios, Ben helps startups disrupt and large organisations to remain competitive by building with Serverless.

name: Ben Kehoe
bio:
Ben Kehoe works in the field of Cloud Roboticsusing the internet to enable robots to do more and better thingsan area of IoT involving computation in the cloud and at the edge, Big Data, and machine learning. Approaching cloud computing from this angle, Ben focuses on developing business value rapidly through serverless (and servicefull) applications.


At iRobot, Ben guided the transition to a serverless architecture on AWS based on AWS Lambda and AWS IoT to support iRobots connected robot fleet. This architecture enables iRobot to focus on its core mission of building amazing robots with a minimum of development and operations effort.


Ben seeks to amplify voices from dev, operations, and security to help the community shape the evolution of serverless and event-driven designs for IoT and cloud computing more broadly.

name: Bhuvaneswari Subramani
bio: Bhuvaneswari Subramani, Chief Cloud Evangelist at Intuitive.ai, is a passionate technophile and strategic leader in multi-cloud migration, application modernization, and end-to-end development. She specializes in cloud service delivery, strategic alliances, and sales enablement, with deep expertise in Infrastructure-as-Code, DevOps, CI/CD, Agentic AI and AI-DLC. She also drives global outreach, mentors leaders, and bridges the gap between industry and academia. A self-made woman in tech, she has been recognized globally: - AWS re:Invent 2018 Diversity Scholar (https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/publicsector/meet-the-women-building-aws-technical-communities-around-the-world/) - AWS Community Hero (2019–2020), AWS DevTools Hero (from 2020 onwards) and featured in AWS Developer Story (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fOURKkUdtv0) - Honoured as AWS Hero of the Year 2022 at APJ Summit, Bangkok - Dr. Werner Vogel's Now Go Build Award 2022 Awardee - Spotlight in AWS Global Summits Video 2025 & early contributor to #AIDLC - Led the Navy Blue team in Road to ReInvent 2025 Hackathon, winning the title with $100K - She co-leads the AWS User Group Bengaluru (https://awsugblr.in/) and AWS WomenInTech India (https://www.meetup.com/aws-user-group-women-in-tech-india/), speaks at TEDx, global tech conferences, and universities, and blogs at https://bhuvana.saryoo.com Connect to collaborate on cloud innovation, leadership, and inclusive tech communities.

name: Brian H. Hough [AWS Hero]
bio: Building epic tech & scaling the cloud at Tech Stack Playbook® AWS DevTools Hero 5x Hackathon Winner 50+ AI, Cloud & Software Clients (work with us ↓) https://techstackplaybook.com

name: Brian LeRoux
bio: Maintaining https://arc.codes since '16. Still the fastest and most stable way to build serverlessly !

name: Brian Tarbox
bio: AWS Hero, AWS Ambassador, Alexa Champion, bAWSton User Group leader, 10 US patents.

name: Bryan Chasko
bio: An inventor committed to modernization utilizing AWS cloud since 2008, Bryan has deployed over 100 AWS services into production, applying expertise in business systems integration, machine learning, industrial IoT, spatial computing, and serverless architectures. By leveraging advanced monitoring and visualization, he reduces costs, fosters growth, and drives improvements across defense, education, retail, agriculture, and space, all under well-architected practices. He has revitalized complex infrastructures; advanced patient care with edge devices, game technology, and augmented reality; and built rugged data pipelines for climate modeling on private satellite networks. Partnerships with Fortune companies, US National Laboratories, healthcare organizations, and universities have hallmarked his collaborative career. Through ambient assistants and AI-driven tools, Bryan furthers accessibility and design for chronic illness and disabilities. He has delivered 1000s of presentations on cloud computing, 3D design, and self-taught learning, and shares locally through AWS User Group Cloud Del Norte (El Paso, TX) & Blender UG NE3D (Boston, MA). Enthusiastic about GenAI, robotics, digital twins, and satellites, Bryan provides compliant solutions for NGOs, startups, enterprises, and underserved markets while navigating regulatory and security needs. His work has been featured in media interviews, product spotlights, AWS Summits, Community Days, re:Invent, and CES.

name: Calvin Hendryx-Parker
bio: Calvin Hendryx-Parker is the co-founder and CTO of Six Feet Up, the premier AI and Python agency in the U.S. Calvin’s Massive Transformative Purpose (MTP) is to inspire and enable tech leaders to open minds and bring the world together for a sustainable future. At Six Feet Up, Calvin establishes the company's technical vision and leads all aspects of the company's technology development. Calvin is passionate about the open-source community and specializes in app development, AI, big data and cloud technology. He is regularly sought after to share his expertise — both at international conferences and in the media. Six Feet Up, a top 10 United States Custom Software Development Company according to Clutch.co, was founded with Python at the forefront. In 2019, Calvin was named an AWS Hero — one of only 50 Heroes in North America. He is the co-founder of IndyPy, the largest Python meetup in Indiana with nearly 2,500 members. In 2023, he was named Tech Executive of the Year by the Indiana Business Journal.

name: Carlos Cortez Bazan
bio: Hello Everyone, Generative AI Startup Founder, AWS Community Hero & AWS User Group Perú Founder. 10+ yrs AWS/Linux, 7+ yrs DevOps, 6+ yrs, +6yrs AI/ML. Specialize in cloud migration, serverless, ML. Content creator, event organizer, writer, podcaster ("Al día con AWS", Imperio Cloud), DeepFridays creator. UGConf Conference creator, hosting 2.5k+ attendees, 105+ speakers. AWS Hero: https://aws.amazon.com/es/developer/community/heroes/carlos-cortez/ Costing and optimization for IT cloud projects that will make you close the best deals. Specially with AWS. Also, great representation for sales management and close dealing with big clients commercial supporting in IT cloud services.

name: Carlos Zambrano
bio: I am a game-changing technology leader with a track record of repeated success at the helm of innovative strategies, tech transformation, and radical problem-solving, propelling growing companies to scale and gain a competitive advantage

name: chaocai
bio: 20+ years of world-class experience in software development, including more than 10 years as a software architect. Currently the VP and Chief Architect at Mobvista Inc. My WeChat public account is: chao-and-software

name: Chris Farris [AWS Hero]
bio: Chris Farris has been in the IT field since 1994 primarily focused on Linux, networking, and security. For the last 8 years, he has focused on public-cloud and public-cloud security. He has built and evolved multiple cloud security programs for major media companies, focusing on enabling the broader security team’s objectives of secure design, incident response and vulnerability management. He has developed cloud security standards and baselines to provide risk-based guidance to development and operations teams. As a practitioner, he’s architected and implemented multiple serverless and traditional cloud applications focused on deployment, security, operations, and financial modeling. He is one of the organizers of the fwd:cloudsec conference (https://fwdcloudsec.org) and has given multiple presentations at AWS conferences and BSides events. He was named one of the inaugural AWS Security Heroes. He is available for cloud security advisory services though his consulting firm PrimeHarbor. When not building things with AWS’s building blocks, he enjoys building Legos with his kid and figuring out what interesting part of the globe to travel to next. He opines on security and technology on Mastodon, Twitter and his website https://www.chrisfarris.com

name: Chris Miller
bio:
Chris Miller is an entrepreneur, inventor, and CEO of Cloud Brigade, an AWS Select partner specializing in Software Development, AI/ML, and IT Ops. After winning the 2019 AWS DeepRacer Summit race in Santa Clara, Chris founded the Santa Cruz DeepRacer Meetup group.


Chris has worked with AWS AI/ML product teams with DeepLens and DeepRacer on projects including The Poopinator, and Whats in my Fridge.


Chris prides himself on being a technical founder with experience across a broad range of disciplines spanning software and hardware, which has led to a lot of crazy projects in competitions and hackathons, such as an automated beer brewery, animatronic ventriloquist dummy, and his team even won a Cardboard Boat Race!

name: Chris Williams
bio: AWS Hero

name: Christian Bonzelet
bio: 👋 Hi my name is Christian. I am an AWS DevTools Hero working as a Solution Architect at DFL Digital Sports GmbH. Based in Cologne (Germany) with my beloved wife and two kids. I am interested in all things around ☁️ (cloud), 👨‍💻 (tech) and 🧠 (AI/ML).

name: Colin Percival
bio:
Colin is the founder of Tarsnap, a secure online backup service which combines the flexibility and scriptability of the standard UNIX "tar" utility with strong encryption, deduplication, and the reliability of Amazon S3 storage. Having started work on Tarsnap in 2006, Colin is among the first generation of users of Amazon Web Services, and has written dozens of articles about his experiences with AWS on his blog.


Colin has been a member of the FreeBSD project for 15 years and has served in that time as the project Security Officer and a member of the Core team; starting in 2008 he led the efforts to bring FreeBSD to the Amazon EC2 platform, and for the past 7 years he has been maintaining this support, keeping FreeBSD up to date with all of the latest changes and functionality in Amazon EC2.


In his spare time, Colin serves as an alumni representative on the Senate of his alma mater, Simon Fraser University, where he frequently brings a perspective from the world of startups to the ivory tower.

name: Cyril Bandolo
bio: Cyril Bandolo is a Data Scientist and Senior Cloud Developer at Serverless Guru, where he leads initiatives to build and deploy scalable machine learning solutions on AWS.

name: Cyrus Wong
bio: Cyrus Wong is an accomplished senior lecturer who oversees the Higher Diploma program in Cloud and Data Centre Administration at the Hong Kong Institute of Information Technology (HKIIT) at IVE(Lee Wai Lee) in Hong Kong. He is a passionate advocate for the adoption of cloud technology across various media and events. With his extensive knowledge and expertise, he has earned prestigious recognitions such as AWS AI Hero, Microsoft MVP - Microsoft Foundry, and Google Developer Expert for Google Cloud Platform (AI) Microsoft Azure AI MVP https://mvp.microsoft.com/en-US/MVP/profile/86da86ff-8786-ed11-aad1-000d3a197333 Google Developer Expert in Google Cloud Platform & AI/ML (GenAI) https://developers.google.com/profile/u/cyruswong Higher Diploma in Cloud and Data Centre Administration https://www.vtc.edu.hk/admission/en/programme/it114115-higher-diploma-in-cloud-and-data-centre-administration/

name: Damian Olguin
bio: AWS Community Hero AWS User Group Leader - UG Cordoba Argentina Co-Founter of Teracloud

name: Damiano Giorgi
bio: Former on-prem systems engineer, I always tend to automate tedious tasks and seek tech innovations. I love Cloud Computing! My only hardware focus now is my bass. If not in the office or band room, check the pub or airport!

name: Daniel Aniszkiewicz
bio: Daniel Aniszkiewicz – Wrocław, Poland Security Hero Daniel Aniszkiewicz is a software engineer with over 10 years of experience in building, architecting, and securing production-grade systems on AWS. His focus spans cloud security architecture, fine-grained authorization, serverless and event-driven systems, and agentic AI systems on AWS - combining hands-on engineering with architectural problem solving. Daniel co-organizes the AWS User Group Wrocław, contributing to the growth and engagement of the local AWS community for years. He is a seasoned conference speaker with multiple appearances at AWS re:Invent in Las Vegas, AWS re:Inforce (including a joint session with the Amazon Verified Permissions service team), AWS Summits in the Netherlands and Dubai, and AWS Community Days in Poland and Prague. His technical work focuses on Amazon Verified Permissions, Cedar policy language, automated reasoning, fine-grained authorization, Amazon Bedrock and AgentCore, serverless and event-driven architecture, OpenTelemetry-based observability, and the security architecture of agentic AI systems on AWS. Daniel shares his work through workshops, blog posts, IaC templates, and open source projects.

name: Daniel Rankov
bio: Daniel Rankov is building and leading Several Clouds to become a premier Cloud and DevOps consulting company. Daniel is in a player-coach role; he has multiple certifications on AWS, including Solution Architect and DevOps professional levels and Security and Networking specialties. Before that, Daniel Rankov built and led the Cloud Center of Excellence (CCOE) team and guided the overall Cloud and DevOps strategy at MentorMate. Daniel's cloud journey began as one of the first people at HeleCloud, now an AWS Premier Partner. There, he became very passionate about AWS and all the benefits that the public cloud brings to businesses. This passion led him to become a co-organizer of the AWS Bulgaria User Group and Sofia HashiCorp User Group. In addition, he now actively speaks at conferences and performs training and workshops on AWS and DevOps. Daniel spent 10 years as a leader of the operations team for Trading212 - a global real-time trading platform building distributed, scalable, and secure low-latency solutions. He worked closely with the development teams and built a holistic view of the Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC). As a result, Daniel embraces the DevOps culture and practices of collaboration towards a shared goal. Daniel believes, "A bad system will beat a good person every time." So, he invests in improving the system.

name: Danielle Heberling
bio: Danielle was an early AWS serverless adopter who joined Stackery, a startup building modern cloud development tools. AWS acquired the company, and the work evolved into Infrastructure Composer in the AWS Console. She's passionate about making cloud development more accessible. She gives talks, writes about her experiences, mentors engineers, and builds products people can depend on and love to use. Danielle co-organizes Believe in Serverless, a Discord community for engineers to learn about serverless and cloud architecture: https://believeinserverless.com/community

name: Darryl Ruggles
bio: Principal Cloud Solutions Architect @ Ciena. With a 25+ year career spanning C/C++ embedded systems, Java application development, ReactJS frontends, microservices, containers, serverless architectures, traditional AI/ML and MLOps, observability, DevOps, SRE, FinOps, and GenAI, I bring a broad perspective to every project. I'm passionate about event-driven architecture, observability, and building solutions with serverless and managed services. I'm a continuous learner who enjoys exploring new tools and techniques. Serverless, AWS, Full Stack, Kubernetes, Rust, Go, Terraform, Security, React, MLOps, Forecasting, Maker, Electronics.

name: Dave Stauffacher
bio: Dave Stauffacher is a Cloud Architect focused on helping organizations THRIVE in AWS. With deep knowledge in data storage and protection, Dave has helped organizations navigate data growth in excess of 30,000%.

name: Denis Astahov
bio: Denis is a Solutions Architect at OpsGuru, where he automates and develops various cloud solutions with Infrastructure as Code using Terraform. Previously Denis worked for Intel Corporation in Israel as an Automation Engineer from 2007-2017. Denis owns the YouTube channel ADV-IT, where he teaches people about a variety of IT and especially DevOps topics, including AWS, Terraform, Kubernetes, Ansible, Jenkins, Git, Linux, Python, and many others. His channel has more than 140,000 subscribers and over 18,000,000 views, making it one of the most popular free sources for AWS and DevOps knowledge in the Russian speaking community. In addition to his industry work, Denis is a Professor at the British Columbia Institute of Technology (BCIT), where he teaches AWS Security. He is also a frequent speaker at international IT conferences, including AWS Community Days and CloudSummits. Denis has more than 16 cloud certifications, including 13 AWS Certifications. He also holds an Master of Science in Technology Management from New York University - Polytechnic School of Engineering.

name: Denis Bauer
bio:
Dr. Denis Bauer is a Principal Research Scientist at Australias government research agency (CSIRO), and adjunct associate professor at Macquarie University. As part of CSIROs digital health research program, she develops machine learning and cloud-based bioinformatics solutions that help fight disease and improve human health. Her open source products include VariantSpark, the machine learning tool for analysing ultra-high dimensional data, which was the first AWS Marketplace health product from a public sector organization, and GT-Scan, the search engine for genome engineering, which was one of the first serverless architectures.


Denis is passionate about facilitating the digital transformation of the health and life-science sector by building a strong community of practice through open source technology, keynote presentations and inclusive interdisciplinary collaborations. For example, the collaboration with her organizations visionary Scientific Computing and Cloud Platforms experts as well as AWS Data Hero Lynn Langit has enabled the creation of cloud-based bioinformatics solutions used by 10,000 researchers annually.

name: Dhaval Nagar
bio: Dhaval Nagar is the Founder & Principal Architect at APPGAMBiT, an AWS-focused consultancy that helps startups and enterprises launch lean, event-driven products. With 19 years in the industry—progressing from developer to architect to AWS Hero—Dhaval has guided teams through every stage of cloud adoption, earning all AWS certifications and serving as both an AWS Certification SME and an AWS User Group Lead. He has spoken at AWS Summit Bengaluru 2024 and AWS re:Invent 2024, where he showcased real-world examples of blending generative AI with serverless workflows to achieve optimal result and scale at optimal cost. Dhaval is committed to democratizing serverless best practices, helping teams cut infrastructure overhead, accelerate delivery, and optimize cloud spend without sacrificing performance or security. When he isn’t sketching architecture diagrams or mentoring builder communities, you’ll find him experimenting with 3D printing or perfecting his barista skills. https://appgambit.com https://antigravityapps.dev/ https://stackadvisor.ai https://ipoiq.in/

name: Dheeraj Choudhary
bio: Senior Cloud, DevOps & AI Engineer with over 10+ years of experience in DevOps / Build and Release Engineering, Software configuration management in automating, build, deploy and release, cloud providers.

name: Dijeesh Padinharethil
bio: I am a dynamic and self-motivated IT professional with over 18 years of experience designing, building, and operating highly available, secure, and scalable cloud and on-premises infrastructure platforms. My expertise spans Kubernetes, AWS and cloud-native technologies, Linux, and open-source ecosystems, with a strong focus on reliability, security, and operational excellence. I am deeply passionate about continuous learning and solving complex infrastructure and platform challenges. My straightforward approach, strong work ethic, and collaborative mindset have enabled me to succeed in solution architecture roles while building trusted relationships across teams and communities. Beyond my professional work, I am strongly committed to giving back to the tech community. I actively organize the AWS User Group Kochi and the CNCF Kochi Chapter, fostering knowledge sharing, mentorship, and community-driven growth within the cloud-native ecosystem. Community Contributions Organizer, AWS User Group Kochi Organizer, CNCF Kochi Chapter Lead Organizer, AWS Community Day Kochi (2019, 2022, 2023, 2024, and 2025 – in-person events)

name: Dimple Vaghela
bio: Active in AWS Community since 2014. Leader of AWS User Groups Ahmedabad & Vadodara. AWS Re:Invent 2024 Awardee AWS APJC Leadership Award in Ownership category. Speaker, mentor, Women-in-Tech India leader, and 2x AWS Certified professional.

name: Dipali Kulshrestha
bio: I am a Cloud & AI leader passionate about building scalable, secure, and intelligent systems on Amazon Web Services. With deep experience in cloud architecture, governance, and enterprise transformation, I focus on translating business problems into production-ready AWS solutions. My current work spans AI, Generative AI, and Agentic systems — designing real-world applications using services such as Amazon Bedrock, serverless architectures, and data platforms. I actively work at the intersection of: Enterprise cloud governance (multi-account strategy, landing zones, security guardrails) AI & Generative AI enablement Agentic AI system design and orchestration Scalable data and platform engineering I enjoy mentoring teams, driving cloud adoption journeys, and building communities that make emerging technologies practical and accessible. My mission: Turn AI ambition into secure, scalable, real-world impact.

name: Dominik Grzywaczewski
bio: Community Hero Dominik Grzywaczewski is a Senior Cloud Site Reliability Engineer at Chaos Gears with more than 15 years of experience in IT. His primary objective is to assist companies in gaining a deeper understanding of Cloud Computing technologies, and effectively leveraging them to drive faster and more secure innovation. Dominik shares his passion by organizing technical meetups and workshops, and consistently collaborates with AWS community members. He also founded the AWS User Group in Lublin (Poland) and co-organizes the AWS Community Day conference in Warsaw (Poland).

name: Drew Firment
bio: Drew Firment is Chief Cloud Strategist at Pluralsight (acquired A Cloud Guru), where he accelerates customer outcomes through skills development. He is an AWS Hero for his dedication to building large-scale learning communities that drive business transformations, and his passion for teaching the world to cloud. Drew was previously Director of Cloud Engineering at Capital One. He led enterprise cloud operations within their Cloud Center of Excellence focusing on migrating the early adopters of Amazon Web Services (AWS). He founded Capital One's cloud engineering college that drove a large-scale talent transformation, and earned a patent for measuring cloud adoption and maturity.

name: Dzenan Dzevlan
bio: Dzenan runs AWS and DevOps consulting company allOps Solutions specializing in high-impact AWS projects, including large-scale migrations, government initiatives, and HIPAA-compliant infrastructure implementations. With over a decade of AWS experience dating back to 2011, Dzenan has managed AWS environments globally, including as a Production Engineering team lead at Yahoo Inc., where he supported the Yahoo Ad Tech platform’s AWS infrastructure, serving millions of users worldwide. Since founding the AWS User Group Bosnia in 2016, Dzenan has significantly expanded the community to over 1,000 members across three user groups. His contributions include organizing online meetups, maintaining an educational YouTube channel, and hosting a free DevOps Mentorship Program aimed at supporting individuals from the Balkans in launching careers in cloud and DevOps. Dzenan also works closely with his sister, Dzenana, in helping South-Eastern Europe community members establish and grow AWS user groups in their regions. His interests focus on AWS, database technologies, and security, holding various AWS, security, and database certifications. He often speaks at various technical meetups and conferences.

name: Dzenana Dzevlan
bio: AWS Community Hero

name: Ed Miller
bio: Senior Principal Engineer at Arm leading technical engagements with strategic partners around ML and IoT. Co-founder and director at the BearID Project, developing open source, ML solutions for non-invasive wildlife monitoring. AWS AI Hero.

name: Efi Merdler-Kravitz
bio: As an AWS Serverless Hero, I bring 15+ years of hands-on development and R&D leadership in cloud technologies. I am the author of Learning Serverless in Hebrew and enjoy contributing to the serverless community through social media, blog posts, open-source projects, events, and user groups.

name: elliott cordo
bio: Elliott is an expert in cloud-native data engineering, strategy, and architecture, with a passion for helping organizations drive value from data. He has more than two decades of experience implementing cutting-edge, data-driven cloud-native applications.

name: Emin Alemdar
bio: Emin is a Solutions Architect at Spacelift and has undertaken tasks, such as producing solutions related to Kubernetes, Cloud technologies, and Cloud Native Transformation. In general, his work focuses on these services and technologies, and he shares best practices with the AWS community. Additionally, Emin is a CNCF Ambassador and is part of the HashiCorp Ambassador Program within the Open Source community.

name: Emrah Samdan
bio: AWS Serverless Hero since 2020, Emrah Samdan co-founded AWS Community Day Türkiye and speaks at events like re:Invent and ServerlessDays. He builds developer tools and leads product at Edge Delta, focusing on observability and AI-driven operations.

name: Eric Hammond
bio: AWS Hero since 2014. AWS enthusiast since 2007. Startup builder since 1996. Code crafter since 1980. Disclosure: Long $AMZN

name: Ernest Chiang
bio: Ernest Chiang is currently the Director at PAFERS Tech, and a seasoned technical leader dedicated to knowledge management, information integration, and productivity enhancement. Since 2008, he started engaging with the AWS cloud ecosystem [1], during which he has continuously deconstructed and restructured various technological architectures. By integrating cloud solutions with business needs, commercial models, and knowledge application strategies, he helps clients enhance operational efficiency and productivity throughout their transformation journeys. Ernest has a solid background in product development and technical management, having worked in the Semiconductor Process Integration Engineering Department (PIE) at TSMC and participated in formulating international Bluetooth communication specifications (FTMS) for the fitness equipment industry, thereby accumulating extensive cross-disciplinary experience in deconstruction and integration. The Ernest PKM [2] personal knowledge system framework he founded fully demonstrates his unique insights in knowledge management and information integration. In 2020, Ernest became one of the mere 200-plus AWS Heroes worldwide, and he continues to refine his technical skills on his ongoing AWS learning journey, leveraging his cross-disciplinary integration abilities to develop productivity-enhancing solutions. [1] https://www.ernestchiang.com/en/awscategories/ [2] https://www.ernestchiang.com/en/posts/2023/ernest-pkm-workflow/

name: Evandro Pires
bio: Evandro Pires is a entrepreneur, technology executive, and international speaker. His journey changed when he gained brutal clarity about the limits he was carrying himself, pushing him to overcome shyness, challenge restrictive beliefs, and develop new strengths in communication, perspective, and complex problem-solving. This transformation led him to become CTO of one of Brazil’s largest technology companies and the first AWS Serverless Hero in Latin America.

name: Ewere Diagboya
bio: Ewere started his career in tech in 2003. He started as a Software Developer in PHP, Visual Basic, HTML, and CSS. He later switched to DevOps and Cloud in 2015. He is the first AWS Community Hero in Africa and a multi-published author. Keynote speaker and Panel speaker at numerous events. He is an AWS Community leader in Nigeria. He speaks about Cloud Computing, DevOps, and innovations around efficient software delivery technologies and processes. He has also been a judge for the Cybersafe Cybergirls Fellowship, PipeOps Hackathon, and Payaza Hackathon. He is the chief editor at EDT (www.ewere.tech), where he writes about AI, AWS, DevOps, and Cloud Computing from the latest announcements in the fields to technical guides and articles about concepts that drive those technologies. EDT is also an open community that allows contributions from other authors. He recently started delving into the tenets of Artificial Intelligence and continues to find ways to implement AI systems in software delivery and efficiency.

name: Faizal Khan
bio: A tech entrepreneur, currently Founder & CEO at Ecomm.in, XiteLogic.com, and CTO at AIProjectPartners.com. I am an ardent contributor to the community through leading the AWS User Group in Hyderabad and talking on AWS services.

name: FAN GUIJU
bio: Cloud-native Application Architect

name: Faye Ellis
bio: AWS Community Hero, cloud architect, keynote speaker, and content creator. I explain cloud technology clearly and simply, to help make rewarding tech careers accessible to all!

name: Filip Pyrek
bio: AWS Serverless Hero / Serverless Meetup Co-Organizer

name: Filipe Barretto
bio:
Filipe is the AWS Global Practice Leader at e-Core, an AWS Consulting Partner since 2013. His main goal is to help companies better understand and use Cloud Computing technologies to innovate faster and securely, following AWS best practices.


He organizes the AWS User Group in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil, promoting talks, hands-on labs, and study groups for AWS Certifications. He also does a lot of work speaking at universities, introducing students to Cloud Computing and AWS services. Whenever he can, he participates in other AWS User Groups in Brazil, working to build a strong and bigger community in the country, and, when possible, in User Groups in other Latin American countries.


His background is in Electronic and Computing Engineering and he is very interested in IoT, serverless, mobile and Alexa, working on several personal projects in his free time which can be found in his GitHub profile.

name: Francisco Edilton
bio:
Francisco is a Site Reliability Engineer at Certain. Previously he worked as DevOps Engineer at Trimble. Before moving to Ireland, he was a Solution Architect at TDSIS, a Brazilian consulting company focused on cloud architecture, software development and network security.


He is an entrepreneur, full-time geek and likes to study every topic related to cloud. With more than 15 years of experience in Network Security and Linux Servers, his AWS studies are currently focused on Python, Serverless and Data Science. Other interests include the stock market, food, and travel.


Francisco organizes the AWS Meetup in Sao Paulo, helping to grow the AWS community and spreading AWS knowledge in Brazil.

name: Franck Pachot
bio: Developer Advocate at MongoDB, AWS Data Hero, PostgreSQL fan,YugabyteDB expert, Oracle Certified Master, and loving all databases

name: Gerardo Castro Arica
bio: AWS Security Hero | Cloud Security Engineer | Founder & Lead Organizer of AWS Security Users Group LatAm I specialize in cloud security for AWS multi-account environments, with a focus on automating security visibility across AWS Organizations. My work sits at the intersection of hands-on engineering and practical security — building real tools that solve real problems in production environments across LATAM. I believe the best way to learn cloud security is by building, not memorizing. Every post I write comes from something I actually built, broke, or discovered in the field. Currently building iam-audit — an open source Python tool that audits IAM users, Access Keys, and root accounts across AWS Organizations, with an interactive HTML dashboard and Docker support. Available at github.com/gerardokaztro/iam-audit As Founder and Lead Organizer of the AWS Security Users Group LatAm, I work to grow the cloud security community across the region — connecting engineers, sharing knowledge, and raising the security baseline one organization at a time. Writing about all of this at Road to CloudSec LATAM → roadtocloudsec.hashnode.dev

name: Gillian McCann
bio:
Gillian is Head of Cloud Engineering & AI at Workgrid Software. Workgrid is a Liberty Mutual startup company focused on delivering an intelligent workplace platform and digital assistant product. Prior to Workgrid, Gillian, who is based at the Liberty IT Belfast office, was Architect of the Liberty Mutual Cognitive Technology team responsible for explorations into how AWS conversational AI could be leveraged to improve the employee experience.


Gillian is a passionate advocate of cloud native architecture with a keen interest in how conversational interfaces, machine learning and AI can be applied within an AWS serverless SaaS architecture to deliver amazing new capabilities. Gillian has presented at AWS re:Invent, ServerlessConf, AWS User Groups, and numerous local events and loves chatting about AWS services & cloud architecture.


Her current interests include: CI/CD leveraging conversation as code, multi modal, multi channel conversational experiences, intelligent search combining NLP, search and machine learning, applying AI to Ops, security and monitoring, and how to Lambda all the things.

name: Gojko Adzic
bio:
Gojko Adzic co-founded MindMup, a collaborative mind mapping application that has been running on AWS Lambda since 2016. He is also the founder of Narakeet, a tool that helps developers, marketers, and educators create narrated videos quickly.


Gojko has published several books, including Running Serverless, the first book focused on AWS SAM. He is also the co-author of Serverless Computing: Economic and Architectural Impact, one of the first academic papers about AWS Lambda.


Together with Aleksandar Simovic and Slobodan Stojanovic, Gojko is currently working on the follow-up book to Running Serverless, titled Realtime GraphQL Applications with AppSync. Gojko frequently blogs about serverless application development on Serverless.Pub and his personal blog.

name: Goran Opacic
bio: #AWS Data Hero and User Group Belgrade @awsugbelgrade leader, CEO of @Estehnet, Databases and Security, deep into #MySQL #Java #Docker and #HTTP since 0.9

name: Gunnar Grosch
bio: Developer Advocate at Sinch | ex-AWS | AWS Serverless Hero

name: Hidetoshi Matsui
bio: Working at Generative Technology Inc. as a manager of engineering. AWS Serverless Hero since 2021. I like building web & broadcasting apps using AWS. I run several branches of JAWS-UG, the AWS community in Japan.

name: Hirokazu Hatano
bio:
Hirokazu Hatano is the founder & CEO of Operation Lab, LLC., which focuses on supporting documentation and education on operational design and cloud operations. Through documentation support, he has published several whitepapers on cloud service operations and MLOps, and provided them to customers.


He has organized the Japan AWS User Group (JAWS-UG) CLI chapter since 2014 and held 165 AWS CLI events over a six-year period, with 4,133 attendees. In 2020, despite the challenging circumstances of COVID-19, he has successfully transformed the AWS CLI events from in-person to virtual: in the first quarter of 2020, he held 12 online hands-on events with 1,170 attendees. In addition, he has organized six other JAWS-UG chapters. He is one of the most frequent JAWS-UG organizers.


Hirokazu has long been focused on the practical and educational usefulness of the AWS CLI and discusses the right knowledge and proper use of AWS at every opportunity.

name: Hiroko Nishimura
bio: Technical Instructor at LinkedIn Learning for "Introduction to AWS for Non-Engineers." Author of "AWS for Non-Engineers." I love writing, photography, and food.

name: Hiromi｜AWS Community Hero
bio: I began as an AWS community leader in 2014. Since then, I’ve been actively involved both in Japan and globally. I’m actively leading a women’s group, a DEI-focused group, and a global community, continuing to foster an inclusive space for new participants and speakers. Feel free to reach out anytime—via DM or on social media, whether you have something in mind or not! Communities I founded and lead as a User Group leader: Global-focused ・AWS Global Commyunity | https://www.meetup.com/ja-JP/awsglobalcommunitygatherings/ Japan-focused ・JAWS-UG DE&I | https://jawsugdei.connpass.com/ ・JAWS-UG Osaka | https://jawsugosaka.connpass.com/ ・Serverless Meetup | https://serverless.connpass.com/

name: horike
bio:
Horike is CEO of Serverless Operations, Inc., developing serverless-focused OSS products and providing serverless solutions for various customers.


He speaks at Japan AWS User Group (JAWS-UG) events, and is active on GitHub, with around 400 contributions in 2020. While working in cloud computing, he participates in and contributes to the open source community, letting people know how fun and meaningful it is by speaking at AWS official events like AWS Dev Day.


Horike has contributed to several open source projects on GitHub, including Serverless Step Functions, Serverless API Gateway Service Proxy, and Serverless Lambda Edge Pre-existing CloudFront. His nearly 40 presentations can be found on SlideShare and Speaker Deck, where people can learn more about his contributions to the community over the past 9 years.

name: Hyunmin Kim
bio:
Hyunmin Kim is a manager in Megazone Cloud's Commercial Technology Center Solutions Architect team. Megazone Cloud started its AWS partner business in 2012 and is currently the largest customer in South Korea. Previously Hyunmin had been developing mobile applications for an LMS (e-Learning, Learning Management System) as an iOS developer for 5 years at 4CSoft. He was then attracted to AWS and moved to a cloud partner. Over the past three years, he has been working with many customers to develop experiences with AWS. Some clients he works with include financial clients such as Shinhan Bank, as well as many startups. Recently, Hyunmin is working on modifying an IoT project (Greengrass) with manufacturing and heavy industry customers.


The AWS community in Gangnam is growing rapidly, and is learning Kubernetes and container orchestration services like Docker and ECS and EKS together. Hyunmin helps organize the AWSKRUG Gangnam as well as the AWSKRUG Container Group, where he frequently presents on various topics.

name: Ian Mckay
bio: Ian is an AWS Community Hero working in Sydney as the Cloud Principal at Kablamo. He is usually focused on cloud architecture, CI/CD and security, but is always trying out the latest AWS services.

name: Ilanchezhian Ganesamurthy
bio: Ilan is currently serving as the Director at Tietoevry, a leading (#1) Nordic Financial and IT service provider in the Nordic region. Ilan AWS UG - Chennai Co-organizer. He is working from AI since 2017. He is AIML & GenAI expert.

name: Ivonne Roberts
bio:
Ivonne is a Software Architect with over fifteen years of software development experience, including ten years working with AWS and more than five years building serverless applications. Her expertise includes developing mission-critical production workloads, researching new technologies and design patterns, building prototypes, and establishing reference architectures.


At Bill.com, Ivonne and her team is responsible for re-architecting, modernizing, and componentizing the application stack, as well as developing scalable and resilient microservices on AWS with an emphasis on serverless. Previously at Edelman Financial Engines, Ivonne and her team pioneered the development and operational readiness of the first microservices from a Java monolith modernization project. With those first microservices, she was able to instruct software engineers on the industry standards they should implement as they began following the company's quest to be all-in on AWS with a serverless-first software development philosophy.


In recent years, Ivonne has begun sharing that industry knowledge with the greater software engineering community. On her blog ivonneroberts.com and her YouTube channel Serverless DevWidgets, Ivonne focuses on demystifying and removing the hurdles of adopting serverless architecture and on simplifying the software development lifecycle.

name: Jacopo Nardiello
bio:
Jacopo is CEO and Founder at SIGHUP, a Milan-based startup focused on Kubernetes, Cloud Native & Container technologies. He started working with Kubernetes and containers back in 2014 and over the years he fell in love with the Kubernetes community and the CNCF as a vendor-independent nexus of collaboration. Since 2017 he is also a CNCF Ambassador.


He regularly speaks at conferences and events, but hes mostly committed towards the local Italian community where he currently runs the Kubernetes & Cloud Native Milano meetup and has been regularly collaborating with AWS and the AWS Milan User Group on all things containers.


He is passionate about developing and delivering battle tested solutions based on EKS, Fargate and OSS software - both from the CNCF and AWS - like AWS Firecracker (the base for lambda), AWS Elasticsearch Open Distro and other projects.


In his free time, either he is with his two sons or streaming StarCraft 2.

name: JaeHyeon Shin
bio:
Jaehyeon is a Site Reliability Engineer at MUSINSA, a Korean online fashion retailer. He joined in the AWS Korea User Group in 2017 and has been a co-owner of the AWSKRUG Serverless Group and Seongsu Group. He was an AWS Community Builder from 2021 to 2024, and mentored new Korea user group leaders, as well as organized AWS Community Days and hands-on labs.


He has technical experience in building applications using AWS Lambda, Amazon EKS, Karpenter, and OpenTelemetry on AWS. He shared his learnings at the AWS Summit Seoul in 2024 and 2022, AWS DevDay Seoul in 2018, AWS Community Day Seoul in 2022,  2021, and 2018, as well as AWSKRUG meetups. Additionally, he is a Python lover and has contributed to grpc_requests, and spoke at PyCon Korea 2020 for building gRPC framework.

name: Jared Short
bio:
Jared is an engineer at Stedi, where they are using AWS native tooling and serverless services to build a global network for exchanging B2B transactions in a standard format. Prior to Stedi, he served as a senior architect and director of innovation at Trek10, an AWS Premier Consulting Partner specializing in IoT and serverless solutions, and Head of Developer Experience at Serverless, Inc.


Jareds work includes early contributions to the Serverless Framework, the underpinnings of what became the Awsume CLI project, co-hosting the Think FaaS Podcast, writings on various blogs about emerging approaches and AWS services, and speaking at conferences. These days, his focus is working with AWS CDK and other toolsets to create intuitive and joyful developer experiences for teams on AWS. When he isnt clouding, Jared likes to pretend he is the worlds okayest video gamer, photographer, husband and dog dad.

name: Jaroslaw Zielinski
bio: One of Poland's leading experts in cloud computing and an independent IT consultant with over 25 years of experience. For over a decade, he has specialized in AWS and VMware solutions and is recognized by international brands such as AWS and VMware for his contribution to education in the field of cloud computing. He holds numerous certifications and is one of the few people in Poland to hold the titles of AWS Community Hero and VMware vExpert. He has worked on dozens of projects related to networks, private and public clouds, cloud migration, and IT infrastructure monitoring. He is the CEO and co-founder of Welastic, a technology company that creates and maintains cloud environments. In his daily work, he focuses on managing large-scale and highly complex cloud projects. In addition, he is actively involved as a trainer, conducting workshops for advanced engineers and companies. He also works as an IT architect and academic lecturer, and is a speaker at industry conferences and author of expert articles. Outside of his business activities, he is involved in promoting the cloud and is the leader of AWS User Group Poland in nine cities. He sits on the program councils of the renowned PLNOG and Cloudyna conferences, and co-creates one of Poland's top events for IT enthusiasts, Cloud & Data Center Day. He is involved in mentoring hackathons and creating conference programs. Privately, he is an enthusiast of IoT, smart homes, and good coffee.

name: Jayesh Bapu Ahire
bio:
Jayesh is a machine learning developer and researcher who enjoys working on distributed neural computers. He is also very active in the Pune, India developer community with leadership roles in the AWS User Group Pune, Elasticsearch User Group, TensorFlow User Group, and Twilio India Community. He started the AWS User Group Pune in 2019 and grew it to over 6,000 members, making it one of the largest tech communities in India.


As an active advocate of AWS, Jayesh has delivered various talks around AWS AI Services and Amazon SageMaker at events such as AWS Community Day Pune 2020 and regular AWS meetups. He has also conducted training for professors and students in various universities in India, including Sandip University, Shivaji University, and Sinhgad College of Engineering. He has authored books on neural networks, reinforcement learning, blockchain, and simulation hypothesis. He is also a technical blogger and has achieved Twilio Champion by Twilio and Most Valuable Blogger by Dzone.

name: Jeremy Daly
bio: Seasoned technology and product leader with 25+ years of experience transforming complex, multi-source data into intelligent, cloud-scale products. Proven record of building and leading cross-functional teams of engineers, data scientists, and product managers to deliver AI-, data-, and cloud-driven innovation at scale. A recognized AWS Serverless Hero, keynote speaker, and developer advocate, trusted for translating cutting-edge research into clear, accessible narratives that align teams and inspire customers. Deep expertise in AWS, semantic data architectures, and large-scale machine-learning systems that drive measurable business outcomes.

name: Jimmy Dahlqvist
bio: Jimmy has been working in tech since 2003 and transitioned into cloud and AWS in 2015. His passion lies in serverless technologies which enables him to solve business challenges and not manage servers. In 2024, he started serverless-handbook.com as the home base of all his serverless adventures. He is an AWS Lead Certification Subject Matter Expert and regularly participates in workshops ensuring AWS certifications are fair for everyone. Jimmy is a well-known voice in the AWS community in the Nordics and he is one of leaders of the AWS User Group Scania. When he’s not writing about serverless, you will find Jimmy talking about his other passions, IoT and BBQ. Leading him to connect his smoker to AWS, in a serverless way of course!

name: Johannes Koch [AWS Hero]
bio: Johannes is a Principal Architect at FICO where he contributes to the FICO®️ Platform. He is a builder at heart and loves to solve problems in different flavors. He believes in full CI/CD automation and everything required to run the solution being written in code. Johannes was in the AWS Community Builders program from 2022 to 2023, he founded the AWS User Group Bergstrasse, helped to start the AWS Community DACH Förderverein, and is part of the team that organizes the AWS Community Day in the DACH region. He also writes about his experiences on his personal blog, and recently started a YouTube channel: @cicdonaws to share experiences and best practices related to Continuous Integration and Continuous Deployment (CI/CD). His mission: empower developers to start their new projects with a true CI/CD pipeline, which allows a quick value generation through automation. He enjoys sharing knowledge as a public speaker, but also helping and mentoring engineers who are starting their cloud journey. He believes in true collaboration within the AWS community to make the individuals successful. Johannes is a proud husband and father. He likes hiking, playing handball, and being outside. If you would like to engage with Johannes, feel free to reach out either in person at conferences like re:Invent, the AWS Community Day DACH, or online through social platforms or E-Mail.

name: John Varghese
bio: John Varghese is an AWS Hero and the AWS Community Lead for the San Francisco Bay Area. A true Renaissance Developer with a career spanning three decades of technological shifts, John specializes in Platform Engineering, IaC, and building for Architectural Resilience. Having navigated the evolution from monoliths to modern cloud-native systems, John brings a deep level of Systems Thinking to every project—understanding the complex feedback loops that define modern distributed environments. As the lead organizer for the AWS Community Day - Security Edition, he champions a Red Team mindset, moving beyond 'vibe coding' to ensure precision and ownership in the age of AI. John is a lifelong Polymath who thrives in high-energy, collaborative environments where the focus is on verification over generation. He is dedicated to mentoring the next generation of builders, proving that the most valuable asset in tech is the human judgment required to design, lead, and evolve.

name: Jones Zachariah Noel N
bio: Jones Zachariah Noel aka Zach Jones is an AWS Serverless Hero and AWS UG Bengaluru leader and at his day job an observability focused Developer Advocate at New Relic. Jones has been an advocate for Serverless first and also best monitoring strategies for applications and infrastructures. Coming from a full-stack developer background has always been a curious on building and breaking things! Jones writes his Serverless stories (blog and newsletter) on The Serverless Terminal and runs a podcast called The Zacs Show Talking AWS with another Hero Zack from NZ. Apart from this, Jones loves to explore the mountains and driving around the country side. And burns money on LEGOs and action figures.

name: Joshua Arvin Lat (ARVS)
bio: Joshua Arvin Lat is the Chief Technology Officer (CTO) of NuWorks Interactive Labs, Inc. He previously served as the CTO of three Australian-owned companies and as the Director for Software Development and Engineering for multiple ecommerce start-ups. Years ago, he and his team won first place in a global cybersecurity competition with their published research paper. He is an AWS AI Hero and has authored several technical books on AI and Security, including Machine Learning with Amazon SageMaker Cookbook, Machine Learning Engineering on AWS, Building and Automating Penetration Testing Labs in the Cloud, and Learning Serverless Security. Because of his proven track record in leading digital transformation within organizations, he has been recognized as one of the prestigious Orange Boomerang: Digital Leader of the Year 2023 award winners.

name: Julia Morgado
bio: Julia is passionate about helping teams build reliable, observable, and scalable systems. At Dash0, she focuses on bridging the gap between technology and people, enabling organizations to understand their systems better and make data-driven decisions with confidence. Beyond her work, Julia is a CNCF Ambassador, AWS Container Hero, Docker Captain, Google Women Techmakers Ambassador, and Girl Code Ambassador. She’s an active community leader who helps organize Kubernetes Community Days New York, AWS Community Day New York, and the CNCF Midtown Meetup NYC, creating spaces where technologists can connect, learn, and grow together.

name: Jungyoul Yu
bio: 🥕 SRE / 🚀 DevOps / 🦸🏻‍♂️ AWS AI Hero

name: Juyoung Song
bio: Juyoung Song is a Director of DAX teams at LG UPLUS. He is currently leading an organization called DAX (DevOps Acceleration), and is working to upgrade S/W organizational culture and technology.

name: Jérémie RODON
bio:
Jeremie is curious, hungry for knowledge and he is a big fan of automation and scripting. His engineering education obviously fixed nothing and consequently he loves working on AWS with the tools allowing him to automate ever bigger things.


Jeremie is a 9x certified AWS Cloud Architect working for Devoteam Revolve, an AWS Premier Consulting Partner. He has 3 years of consulting experience, designing solutions in various environments from small businesses to CAC40 companies and also empowering clients on AWS by delivering AWS courses as an AWS Authorized Instructor Champion. In his old life, Jeremie was the system engineer in charge of the MS Exchange mail servers for 6000+ users, so the concepts of distributed systems, load balancing, high availability, fault tolerance, and data replication were already his daily bread and butter.


His interests lie mainly in security, and especially cryptography: he has done presentations explaining how AWS KMS works under the hood and written a blog post explaining why the quantum crypto-calypse will not happen. He is also an open source enthusiast, writing code to support multi-threaded Python applications using Boto3 and a One Time Password tool to easily manage multiple OTP when working with multiple AWS Accounts.

name: Karolina Boboli
bio:
Karolina Boboli works as an AWS Cloud Architect and Consultant. She has experience in cloud security, cloud governance, cost management, landing zones, serverless, and IoT. As a technical leader and mentor, she is involved in building and guiding cloud teams. She created an online course AWS in practice  your first project about infrastructure as code.


In 2019, she founded a vibrant cloud community, swiatchmury.pl, a Slack for cloud professionals focused on AWS, which she runs on a daily basis. The goal of the community is to have a friendly place to ask questions, inspire each other, and simply be together. From time to time, she gives talks in AWS User Group Poland and organizes her own webinars.


Cloud computing is her second career that has its roots in a business intelligence and accounting process automations project she did for her own accounting and tax advisory company. In her free time, she enjoys riding a bike (off-road), drawing, watercolor painting, dancing, and spending time with old friends.

name: Kaushik Mohanraj
bio: A Cloud Enthusiast with a career spanning roles of Cloud-Native Product Development, Solution Architecting, & Cloud Pre-sales, and now responsible for leading Enterprise Sales for Malaysia Region for a Born in the Cloud Company Blazeclan Technologies

name: Kazuki Miura
bio: aws community hero since 2023

name: Keisuke Usuda
bio: I'm a Senior Solution Architect at Classmethod with CISSP certification, specializing in security consulting and AWS security services, especially advocating for the enablement of Amazon GuardDuty across all AWS accounts worldwide.

name: Ken Collins
bio: Empathy and strategy are my superpowers that connect "hello world" to hello revenue using years of experience in Architecture, E-commerce, Engineering, Infrastructure, Marketing, & Sales. All in on AI and emerging real-world cognitive architectures.

name: Kenneth Attard
bio: Kenneth is an AWS Hero and an Enterprise Architect at Betsson Group in Malta with over 20 years of technical experience . I am also AWS Malta User Group Leader and the organizer of the Malta AWS Community day

name: Kenta Goto
bio: - AWS DevTools Hero - AWS CDK Top Contributor & Community Reviewer - Maintainer of Open Constructs Library - OSS Creator: cls3, delstack, lamver, and some CDK Construct libraries

name: Kesha Williams
bio: Kesha Williams is an award-winning technology leader, AI strategist, and cloud architect with three decades of experience driving innovation at the intersection of artificial intelligence and enterprise systems.

name: Kittaya Niennattrakul
bio:
Kittaya Niennattrakul (Tak) is a Product Manager and Assistant Managing Director at Dailitech, where she works with developer teams, helping them build products on AWS. She loves coding and has fun building new products using AWS. She enjoys sharing with others that coding is more fun, faster, and more secure by using AWS.


Tak has helped lead the AWS User Group Thailand since 2014, and is also one of the AWS Asian Womens Association leaders. She has fun finding new, easy ways to help both developers and non-developers understand AWS, including using pictures to describe AWS Services, and designing basic workshops. In 2019, she and Thailand User Group team, with support from AWS, organized the two big events: AWS Meetup - Career Day (400+ attendees), and AWS Community Day in Bangkok (200+ attendees). Currently, Tak and Vit write a blog for sharing useful information with the AWS community. As of 2021, AWS User Group Thailand has more than 9,000+ members and continues growing.

name: Kohei "Max" MATSUSHITA
bio: IoT Evangelist at Soracom, Inc. My role is to bring IoT to the world through IoT application cases and demonstrations, and I have given over 800 talks since 2017.

name: Konstantinos Siaterlis
bio: Cloud Engineer, AWS Hero, AWS User Group Athens co-organizer, and blogger at thelastdev.com. Passionate about Data and DevOps, with extensive experience in architecting and implementing robust data platforms.

name: Kristi Perreault
bio: AWS Serverless Hero, BelfAWSt User Group & CDK Day Organizer

name: Kristine Armiyants
bio: Leader AWS UG Armenia 3Y, Community Builder x3

name: Kurt Lee
bio: CEO of Mirror

name: Kyuhyun Byun
bio: From scaling chat platforms that connect 50+ million users to building ML pipelines that power intelligent systems—I specialize in creating infrastructure that grows with business needs at massive scale. As ML Data Platform Engineer, I'm currently architecting vector inference systems using Vertex AI and Google Dataflow, while continuously optimizing for performance and cost (recent wins: 80% Redis cost reduction, 20%+ server improvements). My journey as Lead of Chatting for a platform serving 50+ million users taught me that great engineering isn't just about code—it's about understanding user needs, collaborating across teams, and building systems that can evolve under extreme load. Whether migrating legacy platforms to Go microservices or implementing compliance frameworks, I focus on solutions that deliver lasting value at scale. Technical toolkit: ML/AI platforms • Distributed systems • Real-time analytics • Cloud architecture • Organization leadership

name: Lee Gilmore (Serverless Hero)
bio: Lee Gilmore is an AWS Serverless Hero from the UK who specialises in event-driven architectures, the AWS CDK, domain-driven design, and clean code. Lee is also an international speaker, lead architect, writer, OSS contributor, and engineer, with a passion for building enterprise-grade scalable serverless architectures in the cloud, and building high-performing teams based on trust, vision, and empathy. Lee is also the owner of the Serverless Patterns registry with over 100 free open source solutions and detailed articles: https://www.serverlessadvocate.com/patterns and runs a weekly Serverless newsletter: https://serverlessadvocate.substack.com/ Lee also owns https://cloud-cards.com/ which allows users to do micro-learning in their own time, across the categories: software patterns, CICD, engineering, and domain-driven design.

name: Linda Mohamed
bio: I am a Public Speaker, AWS Hero and Consultant focusing on emerging technologies, product innovation and automation. I also run the mixed and Women’s AWS User Groups in Vienna.

name: Linghui Gong
bio: VP of Engineering in Strikingly.com

name: Liz Fong-Jones
bio: Liz is a developer advocate, labor and ethics organizer, and Site Reliability Engineer (SRE) with over two decades of experience. She is currently a Technical Fellow at Honeycomb, and previously was an SRE working on products ranging from GCLB to Flights.

name: Liz Rice
bio: Liz Rice is Chief Open Source Officer at eBPF specialists Isovalent, creators of the Cilium project, and now part of Cisco. She is an award-winning speaker, and the author of O'Reilly books on "Container Security" and "Learning eBPF". She has held many roles with the Cloud Native Computing Foundation, including as a governing board member, chair of the Technical Oversight Committee, and Co-Chair of KubeCon + CloudNativeCon. She was also previously on the board of OpenUK. She has a wealth of software development, team, and product management experience from working on network protocols and distributed systems, and in digital technology sectors such as VOD, music, and VoIP. When not writing code, or talking about it, Liz loves riding bikes in places with better weather than her native London, and making music under the pseudonym Insider Nine.

name: Luc van Donkersgoed
bio: Luc is an AWS Serverless Hero and Principal Engineer at PostNL, where he designs and builds enterprise-scale serverless architectures. He is well known for his articles, presentations, videos, and podcasts about AWS.

name: Luca Bianchi
bio:
Luca is Chief Technology Officer at Neosperience, an AWS ISV company, that delivers a cloud platform to empower brands to understand what makes their customers unique and bring empathy in technology. He has taken a serverless-first development approach to build Neosperience solutions since the very beginning.
