Luca has worked with AWS since 2010 and has developed many cloud-native products to support big data, IoT, and Machine Learning applications. He has more than 15 years of experience in IT, working on microservices and software architectures, and holds a Ph.D. in Computer Vision and Machine Learning.


Luca writes on Medium about serverless and Machine Learning, where he focuses on technologies such as AWS CDK, AWS Lambda, and Amazon Sagemaker. He is co-founder of Serverless Meetup Italy and co-organizer of ServerlessDays Milano and ServerlessDays Italy.


As an active member of the serverless community, Luca is a regular speaker at user groups, helping developers and teams to adopt cloud technologies.

name: Luciano Mammino
bio: Full-stack developer turned cloud architect, Luciano Mammino is an AWS Serverless Hero, author of Node.js Design Patterns (https://nodejsdesignpatterns.com/), and co-author of the e-book Crafting Lambda Functions in Rust (https://rust-lambda.com/). He is also the original author of Middy (https://middy.js.org/), the Node.js middleware engine for AWS Lambda. At fourTheorem (https://fourtheorem.com/), he helps companies be successful with AWS through pragmatic, scalable serverless architectures. He loves building with JavaScript/TypeScript, Rust, and modern cloud systems.

name: Lukasz 'Luke' Dorosz
bio: New tech geek, traveller, and golf freak. AWS Community HERO, AWS Architect & Instructor

name: Lyndon Leggate
bio: AWS AI Hero

name: Maciek Lelusz
bio: Hi, I’m Maciej Lelusz - consultant, architect, and occasional tech therapist. I spend my time wrangling cloud and datacenter infrastructure, convincing organizations that IT transformation isn’t just a buzzword, and reminding people that "lift and shift" is not a strategy. As an AWS Community Hero, I balance hands-on architecture work with community mischief: running meetups, speaking at events, writing blog posts that call it like it is, and podcasting about the good, the bad, and the absurd parts of enterprise tech. I care deeply about creating better experiences with technology - though I may roll my eyes while doing it. Whether it’s storage design or culture change, I’m here to make IT a little smarter, a little more human, and way less boring. Want more insight, or a war story or two? Stay connected: https://lelusz.com

name: Madhu Kumar Yeluri
bio: Madhu is an accomplished technology leader with over 24 years of IT experience and 10+ years in leadership roles, driving digital transformation, cloud migrations, change management, and DevSecOps strategies across finance, telecom, retail, security, and government sectors. He specialises in defining and delivering enterprise-scale cloud architectures, modernising legacy systems, and implementing secure, scalable, and resilient IT environments. With deep expertise in multi-cloud strategies, containerised microservices, and cloud security frameworks, Madhu has successfully built and led global, cross-functional teams, mentoring senior architects and engineers while fostering a culture of innovation and technical excellence. He has developed and implemented robust cloud governance frameworks, ensuring compliance with industry standards and regulatory requirements, including PCI DSS, GDPR, ISO 27001, and SOC2. In addition to his professional achievements, Madhu is also passionate about giving back to the community. Open-source Software (OSS) supporter. He is humbled to serve as an AWS Hero and AWS User Group Leader, DevSecCon Chapter Leader, DevOps Institute Brand Ambassador and Chapter Leader, HashiCorp User Group Leader, Calico Big Cat, and Docker Captain. Blogs: https://medium.com/@cloudgeek7 / https://dev.to/cloudgeek7 GitHub: https://github.com/cloudgeek7 Discord Container Builders: https://discord.gg/TejhNdfe26

name: Manoj Fernando
bio: Manoj is an online teacher and a serverless consultant. He teaches AWS cloud computing on his YouTube channel and on Udemy. In addition to creating videos, he writes technical articles on medium. He is also the leader in AWS User Group Colombo, Sri Lanka

name: Marcelo Paiva
bio: 25+ years of experience in technology and is the CTO at Softprime Soluções, leading product development and research in digital biometrics, facial recognition, and artificial intelligence (AI). With more than a decade working with cloud computing, he specializes in building scalable and innovative solutions using AWS. Passionate about tech communities, Marcelo founded the AWS User Group in Goiânia in 2018, helping grow the local community. Today, he organizes events such as JoinCommunity and Cloud Summit Cerrado, fostering learning and networking in the Cerrado region.

name: Marcin Sodkiewicz
bio: Marcin Sodkiewicz - Principal Software Engineer at Ryanair, AWS Serverless Hero & AWS Community Group Wrocław Leader, Cloud & Serverless Technologies Enthusiast. Mostly focused on architecture and cost optimizations on AWS. Early OTEL adopter.

name: margaret valtierra
bio: I bring hands-on expertise in AWS, cloud migrations, and stakeholder communications. I’ve led Agile and DevOps transformations for large and small teams. I am proud to organize the Chicago AWS user group and AWS Community Day Midwest, a user-run conference for over 400 attendees. I can easily translate ambiguous requirements into actionable milestones, align stakeholders across disciplines, and deliver measurable impact. I’ve also presented at AWS re:Invent, the Chicago AWS Summit, and community events—demonstrating my ability to communicate complex technical concepts with clarity and enthusiasm. AWS Community Day | Midwest Founder and Organizer AWS Community Day | Midwest is an annual one-day, technical conference run for the user community, by the AWS user community. The event has grown from grassroots meetups to a full-scale conference, rotating through Chicago, Columbus, Milwaukee, and now Indy. This work reflects my commitment to building inclusive, high-impact spaces where cloud professionals can connect, learn, and grow. Join us: https://www.midwestcommunityday.com Chicago AWS User Group leader Since 2013 I've been connecting the AWS community in Chicago via events and programs. The Chicago AWS Tech community continues to grow and evolve! Join us: https://www.meetup.com/aws-chicago

name: Markus Ostertag
bio: Markus is Chief AWS Technologist at adesso SE, the leading German IT service provider, and helps customers of all sizes to realize their full business potential through the use of cloud technologies. He loves to leverage the cloud, finding new ways to solve problems and work with cutting-edge technologies. His first contact with AWS was in 2008 while he ran a big German movie review site that specialized in video content. At the same time, he finished his degree in computer science at the Technical University of Munich and had different positions (including CEO, CTO etc.) at companies in the AdTech, MarTech and other areas. He is always eager to work with new AWS innovations and share his knowledge with other companies and users. Markus co-founded and organizes the AWS User Group in Munich as well as the German AWS Community Days events, and he loves to talk about tech (especially cloud, performance, and agility) at meetups, universities, and conferences like AWS re:Invent. Markus is a frequent AWS speaker for more than a decade and has presented multiple times at AWS Summits and AWS re:Invent in Las Vegas. But his most favorite settings are fireside chats with a group of people just discussing tech topics over some food and drinks.

name: Martijn van Dongen
bio: I'm Martijn, I'm passionate about AWS since the early rise of cloud technology. I work for Schuberg Philis as Cloud Evangelist, and I'm a very proud AWS Hero organizing meetups and events for awsug.nl and awscommunityday.nl.

name: Martin Damovsky
bio:
Martin is a Cloud Governance Lead at Ataccama.com, an AWS Partner providing unified data management solutions. Over the past decade, he has been building on AWS as a Software Engineer, DevOps, Security engineer, and FinOps practitioner. Recently he focused on maximizing business value through FinOps, establishing AWS Partner program, and evangelizing AWS cloud within the company. Martin has been particularly interested in AWS Control Tower Account Factory for Terraform, Cloud Intelligence Dashboard, and security and govern tools, such as AWS Security Hub, Amazon GuardDuty and AWS Config.


Martin enjoys sharing his knowledge and experience with AWS communities. He writes a blog, and speaks at meetups, podcasts, and conferences. Additionally, Martin is an AWS User Group Leader for AWS User Group Prague and was an AWS Community Builder from 2023-2024.

name: marumoto
bio:
Kenjiro Marumoto is the founder and president of OPTARC, Inc. Guided by the objective Implement Your Optimal, the company provides consulting and system development services as a cloud integrator.


Kenjiro contributes to AWS user community activities by running Meetups and study sessions for the AWS Hiroshima chapter (about 300 registered members) and the Big Data chapter (about 900 registered members) of JAWS-UG, the AWS User Group network in Japan.


When Kenjiro spoke at the 2019 AWS Summit Tokyo, he received a first place customer satisfaction rating. He was also featured at re:Invent 2018 for his companys Amazon QuickSight use case. In recognition of these activities, he received the 2018 AWS Samurai Award, which is given to users who have made outstanding contributions to JAWS-UG.

name: Masanori Yamaguchi
bio: https://aws.amazon.com/jp/developer/community/heroes/masanori-yamaguchi/

name: Masaya Arai
bio:
Masaya is an 11x certified Tech Lead working for Nomura Research Institute (NRI), an AWS Premier Consulting Partner. He specializes in system design and development utilizing cloud technology for customers in the financial industry.


Masaya is the central organizer of the JAWS-UG Container chapter (about 3000 registered members), an AWS user group in Japan, and he regularly contributes to the activities of the AWS user community.


He also spoke at AWS DevDay Online Japan 2020/2021, giving a presentation about container technology best practices. In recognition of these activities, he is also certified as a 2021 APN Partner Ambassador.


Masaya wrote a commercial magazine called "AWS Container Guide + Hands-on", which became a best-selling cloud-related book on amazon.co.jp, and published more than 10,000 copies. He focuses on promoting development of AWS container technologies through a wide variety of activities such as blogs, public presentations, contributing to magazines, and writing books. He truly enjoys sharing his knowledge and experience with others.

name: Matias Kreder [AWS Hero]
bio: AWS AI Engineering Community Builder | Buenos Aires AWS User Group Leader | AWS Certification SME

name: Matt Coulter
bio:
Matt is a Technical Architect for Liberty IT in Belfast, Northern Ireland. He is focused on creating the right environment for empowered teams to rapidly deliver business value in a well-architected, sustainable and serverless-first way.


Matt has been creating this environment by building CDK Patterns. This is an open source collection of serverless architecture patterns built using AWS CDK that reference the AWS Well Architected Framework. You can simply clone and deploy a pattern, then in the readme you will find all the links to learn more information from AWS Heroes and Advocates plus simple instructions for testing.


Not only has Matt been sharing open source code but he also created CDK Day which was the first community driven, global conference focused on everything CDK (AWS CDK, CDK for Terraform, CDK for Kubernetes and others). Over a thousand people tuned in for the first event which was pulled together in just three months.


In his remaining spare time you may catch him speaking at AWS conferences about CDK and serverless.

name: Matt Lewis
bio: Matt is both an AWS Data Hero and AWS Ambassador, after having worked with AWS in production since 2016. Matt founded and runs the AWS South Wales User Group and is part of the ServerlessDays Cardiff organising team. With a background as a software engineer, he regularly builds out working prototypes of innovative AWS solutions. He has spent significant time growing cloud skills across teams and establishing cloud operating models including Centre of Excellence and FinOps. He is a regular speaker at events including AWS re:Invent and Summits. Most recently this has included talks on agentic AI and how this is impacting on developer experience and application modernisation

name: Matthew Bonig
bio: Cloud Architect at Macedon. AWS DevTools Hero, co-author of The CDK Book.

name: Matthieu Napoli
bio: Hi! I’m Matthieu, open-source developer and consultant. I build Bref, the serverless framework for PHP, and am the author of the Serverless Visually Explained course. I believe running modern applications should be simpler, and I’m trying to do my part!

name: Maurizio Argoneto
bio: I am a CTO/Solution Architect and the founder of the AWS User Group Basilicata. Based in Southern Italy, I have dedicated the last decade to building a thriving tech ecosystem in one of the most remote and beautiful regions of the country. My passion lies in bridging the gap between high-level cloud innovation and local communities. I am the creator and organizer of 'Come To Code', an annual international conference held in the mountain village of Pignola. My mission is to prove that world-class technology can be shared and mastered anywhere, as long as there is a strong human connection behind it. Key Contributions & Expertise: - Community Leadership: 10+ years leading the Pignola Linux User Group (PLUG) and founding the AWS User Group Basilicata. - Event Architecture: Founder and lead organizer of 'Come To Code' (5+ editions), attracting speakers and attendees from across Europe to a small mountain village. - Public Speaking: Regular speaker at international and national conferences (NDC Copenhagen, Codemotion, DevFest, AWS Community Day Italy) focusing on Serverless, App Runner, and Python/Django. - Education & Mentorship: Promoting digital literacy through CoderDojo for kids, cybersecurity workshops in schools, and mentoring students in the 'Cloud Architecting on AWS' program at MT Academy. - Cloud Culture Advocate: Dedicated to showing how AWS services can empower local businesses and young talents in underserved regions.

name: Mayank Pandey
bio:
Mayank Pandey is a cloud architect and teacher, helping both small and large organizations in their cloud adoption journey. His ability to bring forth technical cloud concepts in an easy-to-understand style has made him a go-to person for professionals trying to learn and make a career in cloud.


Mayank began his journey with the AWS Cloud in 2012 and led the development of an end-to-end data warehousing and analytics product on AWS. He has worked with enterprises around topics including cloud automation, IaC, and cloud security. He holds Professional & Specialty AWS Certifications and handles assignments including security & cost optimization on AWS, and cloud-native applications.


Mayank is passionate about teaching and has done several classroom and online trainings. He is an active member of the AWS community and contributes with hands-on demos and video tutorials to the YouTube channel - KnowledgeIndia. The YouTube channel has over 65,000 subscribers and 150+ videos on various AWS topics. Tutorials include topics such as VPC, AWS Transit Gateway, and Amazon ECS.

name: Michael Walmsley
bio: After 30 years of building software, I'm still on the tools - putting bugs in and taking them out, just like everyone else. I co-organize the Melbourne AWS Programming meetup, contribute to AWS Lambda Powertools (Python/TypeScript), and build open-source tooling including MCP servers for AWS documentation, CDK constructs, and serverless patterns published at ServerlessDNA.com. Focus Areas: * Serverless architecture patterns * Infrastructure as code with CDK * AI agent orchestration and MCP I share practical patterns and tooling to make serverless development more accessible to the community. As an innovator and out-of-the-box thinker - it's software, anything is possible.

name: Mike Fiedler
bio: With over three decades of experience as a professional engineer, Mike has amassed a wealth of knowledge and expertise in his field. Throughout his career, he has sought to learn from every colleague he's worked with, and in turn, has taught many others. He has held senior leadership roles at companies such as Datadog, MongoDB, LeafLink, Warby Parker, and Paribus (Capital One) to name a few. Mike has been a speaker at conferences since 2012, and has been recognized for his contributions to the tech community with awards such as the Awesome Community Chef Award in 2016 and an AWS Container Hero since 2018. Currently working as the PyPI Safety & Security Engineer at the Python Software Foundation, he devotes his free time to working on open source tools, learning new technologies, and volunteering as a roller derby referee. With a holistic view of systems and software and a passion for problem-solving, Mike helps others navigate the complexities of the tech world. He can be found on Mastodon https://hachyderm.io/@miketheman, GitHub https://github.com/miketheman, or wearing stripes at a roller derby game near you.

name: Minoru Onda
bio: KDDIアジャイル開発センター株式会社 テックエバンジェリスト。 AWSとAIの分野を中心に、お客さま企業へのプリセールスや技術コンサルティングを行ったり、エンジニア向けの教育サービスを提供しています。 年間数十件の社外講演、カンファレンス登壇を実施。技術ブログや寄稿記事も多数執筆。 2025年12月には、東京にエンジニア600名を集める大型カンファレンス「JAWS-UG AI Builders Day」のオーガナイザーとしてイベントを成功に収めました。 AWS AI Hero、AWS Samurai 2023/2024、2025 Japan AWS Top Engineer & All Certs, Qiita 2025 Top Contributor認定。 著書 - Amazon Bedrock 生成AIアプリ開発入門（2024, SBクリエイティブ） - やさしいMCP入門（2025, 秀和システム） - AIエージェント開発/運用入門（2025, SBクリエイティブ） amazon.co.jp/stores/author/B0D4VSWS9P

name: MLGuy
bio: Helping enterprise companies to benefit from the power of artificial intelligence (AI), including generative AI, and machine learning (ML) in general. Making it easier to experiment with these technologies, educating internal teams on how to achieve success, and supporting the scaling of AI systems into production and widespread usage.

name: Monica Colangelo
bio: I am a technology leader, cloud expert, and AWS DevTools Hero with a strong technical foundation built over two decades in IT. Now Head of Hyperscaler Operations, I bring experience from a career path that started as a system administrator and progressed through DevOps engineering and cloud architecture. As a committed community builder, I lead several technical communities in Italy, organizing the AWS Community Day Italy and managing various AWS User Groups. I also founded The Cloud House, a non-profit organization dedicated to supporting and growing the tech community. As a passionate advocate for diversity in tech, I am dedicated to breaking barriers and creating pathways for women in technology. I speak at international conferences, sharing both technical insights and my experiences as a woman in tech leadership. Through mentoring, writing technical articles, and active community engagement, I work to inspire the next generation of women in technology and promote inclusive tech environments.

name: Namrata Shah
bio: Namrata Shah is an AWS Hero, technology leader, and lifelong learner with over 24 years of experience in the IT industry. Holding certifications such as AWS Certified Solutions Architect – Associate (CSAA), PMP, and Six Sigma, she brings a unique blend of deep technical expertise and strategic leadership. Namrata is passionate about making cloud technologies accessible and actionable. Through her popular YouTube channel, she shares hands-on tutorials and real-world insights across AWS, cloud computing, DevOps, AI, ML, and Generative AI. Her content includes deep dives into Amazon Bedrock, LLMs, serverless architectures, and complete cloud workflows—designed to support both beginners and experienced professionals. Dedicated to empowering architects, developers, and innovators, Namrata emphasizes practical learning, enterprise transformation, and staying ahead in today’s fast-evolving tech landscape. Her motto: Hands-on. Practical. No fluff. With weekly content, she continues to inspire a growing global community—one lab at a time.

name: Nana Janashia
bio: Nana Janashia is Co-Founder of TechWorld with Nana, a top DevOps & cloud e-learning provider. She’s taught millions to become professional DevOps engineers via bootcamps, YouTube, online courses, corporate trainings and hands-on workshops.

name: Nathan Glover
bio: Nathan is a Senior Data Engineer at GovConnex in Perth, Western Australia.

name: Nick Triantafillou
bio: Nick is a Cloud Engineer, Educator, User Group founder, and Christmas Light enthusiast. Having worked as a Linux System Administrator for over 20 years, he discovered AWS in 2009 and hasn't looked back. He was one of the original course instructors at cloud education startup A Cloud Guru, having taught over 1 million students the fundamentals of AWS, and also produced the world's first AWS Certified DevOps Engineer course. He's the founder of his local Wollongong AWS User Group, co-founder of the Sydney Serverless Meetup and has assisted in the planning and operation of both the ServerlessConf and ServerlessDays ANZ conferences. He runs @NickExplainsAWS, where he is attempting to make a video about every single AWS service on Tiktok and YouTube. Each December Nick brings traffic to a stand still by installing over 75,000 LEDs on his house for his serverless, AWS powered light show spectacular: https://twitter.com/AWSCloudANZ/status/1599629849798316035 You can keep up to date with Nick's latest projects via his blog or LinkedIn

name: Nilesh Vaghela
bio: Nilesh Vaghela is an AWS Community Hero Since 2017 and the CEO of Electromech Cloudtech Pvt. Ltd., leading cloud transformation, GenAI innovation, and enterprise modernization initiatives using AWS technologies. With around 28 years of experience in cloud computing, open-source platforms, and IT infrastructure, he has been instrumental in enabling businesses, startups, and individuals to leverage AWS for innovation and growth. -> Awarded “AWS Hero of the Year – APJ Region” in 2023, recognizing his outstanding contributions to -> community growth and knowledge sharing across the Asia-Pacific region. -> Founded the AWS User Group Ahmedabad in 2014, one of India’s earliest and most dynamic AWS user communities. -> Instrumental and active in AWS User Group Vadodara, fostering collaboration and continuous learning across Gujarat’s cloud ecosystem. -> Under his leadership, 12+ AWS Community Days have been successfully hosted across Ahmedabad and Vadodara, bringing together thousands of cloud professionals, students, and AWS enthusiasts. -> Delivered hundreds of workshops, meetups, and technical sessions, mentoring thousands of learners on AWS, DevOps, and Generative AI technologies. Regularly contributes as a speaker, mentor, and jury member at hackathons, AWS events, and community initiatives, inspiring builders to innovate responsibly and effectively on AWS.

name: Niv
bio:
Niv works at Melio where she was the first DevOps engineer at the company, and is now the DevOps Team Lead. She is also co-leader of the AWS Israel User Group, one of the biggest AWS User Groups in the world. As a community leader, she organizes Meetups and ensures they include underrepresented groups in the technology industry. She achieves this by both collaborating with other User Groups and experimenting with new initiatives.


Niv also volunteers as an instructor in OpsSchool, which is a non-profit program meant to gather industry leaders to contribute together, train new DevOps engineers, and help the community continue the cycle of good deeds. She is active in tech user groups, forums, and Meetups, and is committed to sharing her knowledge and experience at any given opportunity.

name: Noah Gift
bio: Founder of Pragmatic AI Labs and educator to millions of learners on Coursera, edX, O'Reilly, and Pearson. I build and teach production-grade AI and cloud content — with a focus on MLOps, generative AI, Rust, and data engineering — alongside a growing catalog of AWS-centric specializations covering Amazon Bedrock, Amazon Q, SageMaker, and serverless AI on Lambda. I lecture at Duke University (AI Product Innovation EIR) and have taught at UC Berkeley, UC Davis, and Northwestern. Author of several books with Pearson and O'Reilly, including Pragmatic AI, Python for DevOps, and Implementing MLOps in the Enterprise. My goal is simple: make production AI accessible to practitioners who need to ship, not just experiment. If you're learning AWS and want hands-on paths through foundation models, prompt architecture, or deterministic LLM programming, let's connect.

name: Nora Schoener (wolkencode)
bio: Nora is a Senior Cloud Engineer with a long-standing career in the tech industry. Throughout her career, she has held various roles, from Software Developer to Architect and Tech Lead. She mainly focuses on Cloud Computing designing architecture and incorporating DevOps and Site Reliability Engineering principles to help development teams maintain healthy, reliable application environments. With a passion for Infrastructure as Code, Nora loves making backend systems visible and understandable for both developers and stakeholders alike. Nora speaks at international and local conferences and meetups. She is known for her fun, vibrant presentations and outgoing style that connects the dots for her audience. As a community lover, Nora has been active in AWS User Groups since 2016. Nora co-organizes the AWS User Group Nuremberg and contributes to the AWS Community DACH Support Association. She also founded She 'n IT Nuremberg, a meetup that connects women in tech. Nora blogs at wolkencode.de, where she combines cloud technology, her passion for manga art, and sharing glimpses of her everyday life. https://wolkencode.de/ https://www.meetup.com/nurnberg-aws-user-group https://sheandit.de/

name: Olalekan Elesin
bio: With over a decade of experience leading innovative teams, I've consistently driven results across SaaS, Cloud, Data, and AI domains. I'm passionate about sharing knowledge, particularly in building AI & ML on AWS Cloud as Machine Learning Community Hero

name: Or Hiltch
bio: Or Hiltch is VP of Engineering at JLL Technologies, a Fortune 500, global commercial real estate services company with 80 offices around the world and 100,000 employees.

name: Paloma Lataliza
bio: Paloma is a Cloud Engineer who loves sharing knowledge. She leads the AWS User Group Minas Gerais, organizes the AWSome Women Community Summit Brazil, and founded the Mulheres na Nuvem Minas Gerais (Women in the Cloud Minas Gerais) project. Formerly an AWS Community Builder, she has produced technical content, spoken at Cloud and DevOps events, and mentored professionals looking to deepen their technical expertise. Within the AWS community, she discovered the true meaning of community, which inspired her commitment to transformative causes, such as supporting people in socially vulnerable situations and advancing women in STEM. She dedicates her time to helping more women enter tech by creating support networks and offering free classes and mentoring to guide them on their journey. Paloma continues to build a strong career as a Cloud Engineer while making a positive impact on the women in tech community.

name: Paolo Latella
bio: Helping Organizations Unlock Value by Pushing Digital Innovation, from People to Edge Technologies | Cloud Strategy & Governance | Platform Engineering | Generative AI Explorer | AWS Hero | AWS Authorized Instructor

name: Park SangUk
bio:
SangUk Park is a Chief Solutions Architect at Megazone, which became Korea's first AWS Partner in 2012 and is the only AWS Premier Consulting Partner to provide AWS support in Korean.


He served as a System Architect for KTs public cloud and VDI design, and led the system operation of YDOnline and Nexon Japan, one of the leading online gaming companies. Certified both as an AWS Solutions Architect - Professional and AWS DevOps Engineer - Professional, SangUk has authored AWS books, including DevOps and AWS Cloud Design Patterns, and translated four books related to the AWS Cloud.


Hes been making efforts to revitalize the local AWS Korea User Group community as co-leader by presenting at AWS Korea User Group meetings and AWS Summits, and helping to establish small group gatherings such as the AWSKRUG System Engineers in Gangnam. Also, he has done many hands-on labs and has been running a booth as a leader of the user groups at AWS events to cultivate developers and system engineers.


SangUk maintains a close relationship with the Japanese AWS User Group (JAWS UG), using his excellent Japanese communication skills and experiences in Japan. He makes every effort to participate in events held between Japanese and Korean user groups as a facilitator and translator, and will promote cross-regional communications beyond APAC going forward.

name: Parthasarathi Balasubramanian
bio:
Parthasarathi (Partha) is a Cloud Solution Architect at 8K Miles, helping large Fortune 500 pharmaceutical and life science customers build highly available, scalable, and secure cloud infrastructure for their application deployments in the AWS Cloud.


Before joining 8K Miles, he worked at AstraZeneca and Logitech as a Cloud Infrastructure Engineer focused on cloud architecture, migration, and network security. As a cloud enthusiast who has been an AWS user since 2013, he holds the AWS Certified Solution Architect Professional & Certified Security Specialty certifications. Currently he is focusing on DevOps, serverless, containers, and Kubernetes on AWS.


Partha founded the AWS User Group Chennai in 2018. Currently it has 3000+ active members, and continues to interest and attract many in the cloud space. He regularly organizes AWS User Group meetups as well as the first-ever AWS Community Day Chennai 2019, which was a grand success, with 450+ participants. He also started the AWS User Group India Facebook page for organizing live webinars, which has attracted 1100+ followers within a span of two months time.


Partha regularly speaks at AWS User Group meetups, delivers guest lectures, and conducts workshops on AWS Cloud computing for students at engineering colleges and universities in Chennai.

name: Pascal MARTIN
bio: Principal Engineer, 🌩️ DevOps. 🎤 Speaker. 📚 Author of “Votre Première Conférence”, “The Copenhagen Initiative”. AWS Container Hero. 🖋️ Currently writing “Découvrez AWS, en un mois” 📖. Messages+opinions are my own. FR/EN.

name: Pawel Zubkiewicz
bio: Since his first encounters with AWS cloud in 2016, Paweł has been fascinated by serverless solutions. Quickly he realized that the cloud-native approach was the future of IT and decided to change his career path to pursue that direction. In 2018 Paweł started Serverless Polska, an online community for serverless enthusiasts, where he shares his technical knowledge and introduces serverless to a broader audience. Shortly after, he began publishing a newsletter about serverless and AWS cloud. He continuously shares his expertise and insights with the Polish-speaking community to this day, both online and as a conference speaker. In 2020 he was chosen for the prestigious AWS Community Builders program and started sharing serverless tutorials. Apart from serverless architecture, Paweł also specializes in AWS cost optimization. Paweł works as a Cloud Architect and Cost Optimization Consultant, helping companies build products on AWS in a cost-effective manner. He lives in Wroclaw, Poland, with his wife and a dog called Pixel. He's an avid mountain biker and a traveler.

name: Peter Sankauskas
bio: Peter has been part of the AWS Community for over a decade, growing the Advanced AWS meetup (user group) in San Francisco, contributing to and created multiple open source projects, and spoke an many conferences across USA. As a day job, he is a Portfolio CTO at Caylent, looking after enterprise accounts. In his spare time, he runs the annual Answers for AWS community survey and provides strategic guidance to startups through PAS Ventures.

name: Peter Sbarski
bio:
Peter Sbarski is VP of Education & Research at A Cloud Guru and the organizer of Serverlessconf, the worlds first conference dedicated entirely to serverless architectures and technologies. His work at A Cloud Guru allows him to work with, talk and write about serverless architectures, cloud computing, and AWS. He has written a book called Serverless Architectures on AWS and is currently collaborating on another book called Serverless Design Patterns with Tim Wagner and Yochay Kiriaty.


Peter is always happy to talk about cloud computing and AWS, and can be found at conferences and meetups throughout the year. He helps to organize Serverless Meetups in Melbourne and Sydney in Australia, and is always keen to share his experience working on interesting and innovative cloud projects.


Peters passions include serverless technologies, event-driven programming, back end architecture, microservices, and orchestration of systems. Peter holds a PhD in Computer Science from Monash University, Australia.

name: Philipp Garbe
bio: Living in Bavaria. Working in the Cloud. AWS Container Hero

name: Prashanth HN
bio: AWS Serverless Hero, Co-founder & CTO of AntStack

name: Rafał Mituła
bio:
Rafa is a Cloud Data Engineer and Architect within the Data & AI division at Chaos Gears. He is always eager to tackle new challenges and find innovative and effective solutions. This dedication extends to his goal of constantly expanding his knowledge and proficiency in solutions architecture.


Rafa is actively involved in the AWS community, co-organizing the AWS User Group Warsaw meetups and the AWS Community Day Poland conference. Besides his technical and organizational roles, Rafa shares his expertise by speaking at conferences and leading workshops aimed at introducing newcomers to AWS and data analytics, such as the AWS Data Engineering Immersion Days. In addition, Rafa creates content about AWS and data engineering, which he shares on the Chaos Gears blog and his LinkedIn page.

name: Ran Isenberg
bio: Ran Isenberg is an AWS Serverless Hero, a Senior Principal Software Architect at Palo Alto Network, and a consultant specializing in AI adoption, Platform Engineering and Serverless. With 15+ years of experience in software development and security, Ran has spent the last 6+ years designing and building customer-facing SaaS Serverless services, ensuring the highest production readiness and security standards. Ran maintains a website - RanTheBuilder.cloud where he shares his knowledge and speaking sessions. Ran contributed several utilities to the Powertools for AWS Lambda open-source project and maintains numerous serverless relate open-source projects on his GitHub account. One of these projects is the AWS Lambda cookbook – a serverless service template that gets you started in the serverless world with all the best practices in seconds. Another project is the aws-lambda-env-modeler, a Python library designed to simplify the process of managing and validating environment variables in your AWS Lambda functions. On a personal note, Ran is a proud father of two boys, an amateur barista, and plays the electric guitar. Sessionnize profile: https://sessionize.com/ran-isenberg/

name: Ran Ribenzaft
bio:
Ran Ribenzaft is the Chief Technology Officer at Epsagon, an AWS Advanced Technology Partner that specializes in monitoring and tracing for serverless applications. Ran is a passionate developer that loves sharing open-source tools to make everyones lives easier and writing technical blog posts on the topics of serverless, microservices, cloud, and AWS on Medium and the Epsagon blog.


Ran is also dedicated to educating and growing the community around serverless. He organizes Serverless meetups in SF and TLV, delivers online webinars and workshops, and frequently gives talks at conferences. Ran welcomes anyone who wants to learn more about building on serverless. Whether you need help taking your first steps getting started with serverless or you want to dive in to complex architectures, he invites you to contact him through Twitter and start a conversation.

name: Raphael Quisumbing
bio: Helps tech and cloud enablement to multitude various brands, enterprises and online startups. Recognized Cloud Enthusiast and leader (AWS Hero) with genuine enthusiasm for effective mentoring. Comes from a family of teachers and educators, serving in the academe as well as being an AWS Authorized Instructor. As co-leader of the AWS User Group Philippines since 2013, regularly answers questions, gives advice and organizes events for the AWS community.

name: Ray Goh
bio: Building on AWS since 2017. Past AWS DeepRacer and AWS AI League winner. AI/ML community leader ("The Gen-C") in Singapore.

name: Ray Lin
bio: Ray has been working in the software industry for nearly two decades, specializing in building teams and developing new products from the ground up. His core expertise includes information security and auditing, DevSecOps, AI, AWS cloud architecture design, BA and SA, software project management, and agile SaaS product development. Ray is a recipient of the Taiwan Inventor Creativity Award and has also been recognized with the Rising Star Professional Award and as a Cybersecurity Career Mentor by EC-Council. He has held cross-functional executive roles across a diverse range of organizations, including companies in the United States, Japan, and Taiwan, as well as startups, publicly listed enterprises, and multinational corporations. In addition to holding more than 50 international professional certifications in cybersecurity, cloud, AI, project management, and agile, Ray is also the first AWS Security Hero in GCR and among the world's first cohort. He currently serves as President of DevSecOps Taiwan and CEO of the PMI Taiwan Awards Office, where he is responsible for organizing the Project Management Grand Award, widely regarded by the media as the "Oscars" of project management in the Chinese-speaking world. To date, he has also translated and published seven well-known books on agile methodologies.

name: Rehan van der Merwe
bio:
Rehan is a developer, architect, and AWS junkie at heart, while consuming an unhealthy amount of coffee, he focuses on serverless and all that AWS has to offer.


He organizes the AWS PTA Meetup and does the occasional presentation as well. He is an avid blogger helping the AWS community where possible, always lurking in all the #aws slack channels and answering questions.


Rehan has a strong background in Electronic and Electronic Communications Engineering, but has shifted focus to everything AWS. He has/have held multiple AWS certifications (including AWS Certified Solutions Architect  Professional) and is passionate about serverless and architecting for big data and microservices.


His favorite language is TypeScript but he is multilingual and has touched on many industries and technologies. Where he has worn many different hats like; backed developer, full stack developer, architect, platform, SRE, DevOps, and team lead.

name: Renato Losio
bio: Renato has extensive experience as a cloud architect, tech lead, and cloud services specialist. Currently, he lives in Berlin and works remotely as a principal cloud architect. His primary areas of interest include cloud services and relational databases. He is an editor at InfoQ and a recognized AWS Data Hero. You can connect with him on LinkedIn.

name: Ricardo Ceci
bio: Apasionado por cloud computing y desarrollador web con más de 15 años de experiencia. Disfruto emprender, crear cosas y hacer que los equipos funcionen. Mi encanta definir procesos y encontrar soluciones

name: Richard Fan
bio: Richard is a Security Engineer at ExpressVPN. He is dedicated to helping people easily adopt AWS, and supports them with moving workloads to AWS, promotes best practices, and streamlines cloud governance. The experience Richard has gained over the years has allowed him to focus more on making security on the cloud easy. Richard has developed different tools to simplify the experience with AWS security services for users. He has contributed to the AWS Nitro Enclaves community since its early stage. The nitro-enclave-python-demo project has helped many builders get started on AWS Nitro Enclaves, and it has also been adopted by some AWS workshops. Furthermore, Richard promotes the concept and use cases of enclave technology by partnering with multiple companies to review their AWS Nitro Enclaves offering.

name: Rob Koch
bio: Rob is a tech enthusiast who guides projects from concept to completion with expertise in event-driven architecture and real-time data utilization. He excels at analyzing systems and mapping transitions to event-driven frameworks, backed by strong enterprise data strategy experience. His versatile skills in software development, database management, Kubernetes, security, analytics, and enterprise architecture have served clients across energy, healthcare, finance, insurance, marketing, telecommunications, and non-profit sectors.

name: Rola Dali
bio: I am a problem solver with tools optimized for data, ML and AI loads. I have a PhD is neuroscience and bioinformatics and have moved from academia to industry. I help architect and build complex systems that revolve around data.

name: Rolf Koski
bio: Rolf is Co-founder and CTO of Elexive, an AI-native leadership services company helping good leadership turn into Great Leadership. He is focused on building AI-assisted workflows that democratize access to best business leadership practices and data. He has a track record as one of the leading cloud transformation experts in the Nordics and has 18+ years of actual concrete AI experience. He sees community contributions as a valuable asset and founded AWS Community Day Nordics and leads AWS User Group Finland, one of the largest IT communities in the Nordics. Rolf is known for his pragmatic approach to making complex technical concepts actionable, bridging business leadership, company boards, developers, and makers. As "The Pragmatist Futurist," he champions practical approaches that make advanced technology accessible and actionable.

name: Rosius Ndimofor
bio: Software Engineer with over 10yrs of Mobile and Web Developers. Created a learning platform to Master Cloud and AI through hands-on workshops.

name: Rossana Suarez 🔥Roxs🔥
bio: I’m Rossana Suarez (roxsross), Tech Lead DevOps and creator of the #90DaysOfDevOps challenge an initiative that empowers the community to learn DevOps through real practice, collaboration, and a spark of fun .

name: Rowan Udell
bio: 15+ years building on AWS, from early startup days through enterprise transformations. I've led engineering teams, scaled cloud practices, and built mission-critical systems. Author of "The Practical AWS IAM Guide" and AWS Administration Cookbook (Packt). I help scale-up startups pass their first major compliance audits (SOC2, PCI, ISO) with ease.

name: Rustem Feyzkhanov
bio: Rustem Feyzkhanov is the Engineering Manager for the Snorkel AI Platform team, building data-centric AI solutions. His background includes roles as ML Engineering Manager at Instrumental (manufacturing AI) and an engineer at Astro Digital (satellite imagery AI). A prominent voice on leveraging cloud infrastructure for AI, Rustem is the author of the book “Serverless Deep Learning with TensorFlow and AWS Lambda.” He is a seasoned speaker who has shared his expertise at numerous prestigious conferences, including O'Reilly Strata, O'Reilly Software Architecture, and AI DevWorld. His presentations focus on the practical application of cloud-native AWS services for building efficient and scalable deep learning training and inference pipelines. In addition to his professional and speaking engagements, Rustem is the creator of several popular open-source repositories on GitHub that provide valuable resources for the community. His current interests lie in pushing the boundaries of serverless for AI/ML, tackling the unique challenges of hosting large language models (LLMs), and engineering robust, production-grade ML workflows using AWS infrastructure.

name: Sabiha
bio:
Sabiha Ali is a Solutions Architect at ScaleCapacity. She specializes in Amazon Connect, Architecting Resilient, and Secure systems in the cloud. As an Amazon Connect Ambassador, she helps businesses enhance their customer experiences. Her unwavering passion for learning has earned her numerous AWS certifications (9X), solidifying her expertise in the field.


Before transitioning into the tech industry, Sabiha was a passionate Mathematics teacher. Inspired by her love for logical problem-solving, she made an impressive 180-degree career change to become a Solutions Architect. Her background as a teacher equips her with strong communication skills and an ability to simplify complex concepts. She firmly believes that anything is possible, that it is never too late to pursue ones dreams, and one can overcome any challenge and embark on a fulfilling career path.


Starting out as an active AWS Community Builder, she became an AWS User Group Leader in Dubai, and has helped create a vibrant and one of the most active communities of AWS users around the globe. Sabiha is also committed to empowering women in the tech industry, making her a valued professional and an advocate for change.

name: Sanchit Jain
bio: Sanchit Jain is a Lead at Quantiphi Inc. and a certified AWS Cloud expert with deep expertise in building secure Enterprise Data Platforms. He plays a pivotal role in leading the AWS User Group Mumbai, driving community engagement and talent development through knowledge-sharing sessions and university initiatives. A recognized speaker at global forums such as AWS re:Invent and AWS Summit India, he also shares thought leadership via technical blogs on AWS services. 1. Blogs - https://blog.getsetbuild.cloud/ 2. LinkedIn - https://www.linkedin.com/in/sanchitdilipjain/ 3. AWS UserGroup Mumbai - https://www.linkedin.com/company/awsugmum 4. AWS UserGroup Data - https://www.linkedin.com/company/awsugdataindia 5. Hashicorp UserGroup Mumbai - https://www.linkedin.com/company/hug-mumbai

name: Sandip Das
bio: Senior Cloud & DevOps Engineer | Kubernetes Expert | SRE, Platform Engineering, MLOps & AIOps Practioner | AWS Container Hero | Teacher | Mentor

name: Sang Chris Park [AWS Hero]
bio: I’m a developer deeply focused on serverless technologies, handling backend/frontend development, AWS infrastructure design, and DevOps. As a Dev Lead, I guide my team to build high-quality products through scalable and efficient serverless solutions.

name: Sathyajith Bhat
bio: Sathyajith Bhat is an experienced Cloud Architect & SRE with over 17 years of experience in Cloud Architecture, DevOps, Site Reliability Engineering. His expertise includes AWS, Azure, Kubernetes, Docker, Chef, Python, TypeScript, Linux, Bash, Docker, Terraform, Prometheus and more. He is not fanatic about specific tools or services and believes in making the most available out of the tools he gets to work with. He is the author of [Practical Docker with Python](https://amzn.to/3B9pQaz), a co-author of [The CDK Book](https://www.thecdkbook.com/) and is recognized as an [AWS Container Hero](https://builder.aws.com/community/@sathyabhat)

name: Scott Hsieh
bio: Scott Hsieh, also known as Shu-Jeng Hsieh, is a Lead Data Architect at 104 Corporation where he promotes, plans, and implements a lakehouse paradigm into 104 Corp. within hybrid environments to accelerate data engineering, data analysis, and data science.

name: Sebastian Korfmann
bio:
Sebastian Korfmann is an entrepreneurial Software Engineer based in Hamburg, Germany, where he helps tech companies deliver innovative projects. With a current focus on Cloud Tooling, Infrastructure as Code, and the Cloud Development Kit (CDK) ecosystem in particular, he has over a decade of experience in software engineering and technical leadership. Recent projects have included a large-scale GraphQL implementation for a German listed company and a cloud migration of an on-premise microservices platform to Amazon Web Services.


Sebastian is a core contributor to the CDK for Terraform project, which enables users to define infrastructure using TypeScript, Python, and Java while leveraging the hundreds of providers and thousands of module definitions provided by Terraform and the Terraform ecosystem. With cdk.dev, Sebastian co-founded a community driven hub for all things CDK and he runs a weekly newsletter covering the growing CDK ecosystem.

name: Sena Yakut
bio: Sena has experience in understanding and applying security concepts for cloud platforms. She defines security needs for cloud designs, provides security services on AWS, and manages risks for cloud solutions. Sena is recognized as an AWS Security Hero for her expertise and is also one of the organizers for AWS Community Day Turkiye, demonstrating her commitment to building and supporting the cloud community. She is skilled in creating secure cloud architectures, implementing best practices, and ensuring compliance with security standards. She has a strong background in incident response, threat analysis, and vulnerability management. Sena is also proficient in automating security processes and integrating security tools to enhance the overall security posture of cloud systems.

name: Serhat Can
bio: Serhat is a passionate technologist, community leader, and co-founder & product development director at JumpCloud. Prior to his current role as Director, Product Management at JumpCloud, he served as the co-founder and CEO at Resmo. Serhat also holds the prestigious title of AWS Community Hero, acknowledging his expertise and contributions to cloud computing. He enjoys delving deeply into every aspect of building outstanding products. Hes currently based in Ankara, Turkey.

name: Serkan Özal
bio:
Serkan Ozal is the CTO and founder of Thundra, a serverless-centric debugging, monitoring, and security solution for applications. He has 10+ years of expertise in software development and is a Ph.D. candidate at METU. He is an AWS DevOps Pro Certified Engineer and has a patent on distributed environments. He mainly works on serverless architectures, distributed systems, and monitoring tools.


For years, Serkan has published some of his work as open-source tools and libraries on his GitHub account to be used and contributed to by the community. Serkan writes technical blog posts both on his Medium account and the Thundra blog regularly. He also actively shares his thoughts (mostly about AWS, serverless, and monitoring), research, and works on many social media platformsyou can contact him through Twitter to talk about serverless, microservices, monitoring tools and distributed systems, or to just say Hi.


In addition to his responsibilities at Thundra, Serkan speaks at international conferences and moderates serverless workshops. Hes also a co-organizer of the Cloud and Serverless Turkey Meetup and was part of the team that organized ServerlessDays Istanbul in 2019.

name: Shaoyi Li
bio: Shaoyi Li is a Lead Cloud Engineer focusing on cybersecurity and GenAI, advocating for cloud GenAI security and governance solutions to help the community build secure, compliant, and responsible GenAI applications. He partners with the security community to reinforce the mindset that "security is job zero." He has obtained 15x AWS Certifications and 88x AWS Badges, demonstrating his enthusiasm and professional expertise in AWS. Shaoyi Li is a regular speaker at AWS events, such as AWS re:Invent, AWS re:Inforce, AWS Summits, AWS media events, AWS Community Days, and AWS User Group Meetups. He shares his insights in AWS case studies, AWS blogs and social media. His impactful content has attracted over 145k followers, 20 million views, and 1.5 million likes across his channels. He received the 2023 & 2024 Annual Best Blogger and Best Certification Ambassador from AWS T&C, was recognized as the best AWS Hero of the Year in China 2024 and selected by Jefferson Frank as one of the “Top 20 Global AWS Influencers to Follow.” As an AWS User Group China committee member, Shaoyi Li supports the members by helping them deepen their AWS expertise to deliver more high-quality events to the community. His cloud journey never ends, much like his commitment to the AWS community. His dedication, hard work, and persistence in his AWS cloud journey have inspired many developers from underrepresented regions to launch their cloud journeys and contribute their knowledge back to the community.

name: Sheen Brisals
bio: Sheen is a technologist, speaker, and co-author of 'Serverless Development on AWS: Building Enterprise-Scale Serverless Solutions' (O'Reilly, 2024), and author of 'Speak Effectively At Conferences.' As an experienced engineering leader, Sheen guides enterprise teams in cloud adoption.

name: Sheyla Leacock
bio: Security Hero Sheyla Leacock is an IT security professional, mentor, technical author, and international speaker contributing to the global cloud and cybersecurity community. She has spoken at AWS Summit Mexico, AWS Summit LATAM in Peru, and led PeerTalk sessions at AWS re:Invent, while also leading the AWS User Group in Panama and regularly participating in AWS Community Days and regional meetups. Beyond AWS-focused events, she has delivered talks at more than 20 international conferences and publishes technical articles and educational content on AWS cloud computing and cybersecurity. She collaborates with universities as a guest lecturer, supporting the development of emerging technology and cybersecurity talent. Through community leadership, knowledge sharing, and education, she contributes to strengthening the AWS and cybersecurity ecosystem.

name: Shigeru Oda
bio: AWS Community Hero / Japan AWS User Group Beginners Branch Manager Utilize S3, S3 Tables, and Apache Iceberg to perform analysis and gain insights. Aim to build infrastructure for discovering valuable data.

name: Shimon Tolts
bio: Co-Founder and CEO of Copperhelm Previously as the CEO and Co-Founder of Datree, a Y Combinator-backed startup, I lead a team of engineers and developers who are on a mission to prevent misconfigurations in Kubernetes, the most popular cloud-native platform. With over five years of experience in building and scaling Datree, I have developed a deep expertise in Kubernetes security, cloud-native technologies, and AWS services. I am also passionate about sharing my knowledge and insights with the global cloud community. I am a CNCF Ambassador, a co-organizer of the Israeli chapter and KCD events, and an AWS Community Hero, leading the Israeli community with 8,000 members. I co-organize UpWards PLG, a founder group for product-led growth startups. I enjoy writing and speaking about cloud-native best practices, AWS optimization, and Kubernetes challenges.

name: Shingo Yoshida
bio: AWS Serverless Hero / CEO @ Section-9 / Co-Founder COO @ Generative Agents / A Founder of (Serverless | ChatGPT | LangChain) Community(JP)

name: Shun Yoshie
bio: Please feel free to follow and DM. # Work - Nomura Research Institute, Ltd # Job/Role - Security Consultant # Award - AWS Samurai 2018 - AWS Samurai 2019 - AWS Samurai 2020 - AWS Community Builder (Security) (2020) - AWS Community Hero (2021) - AWS Security Hero (2023) - APJ User Group of the Year (2023) - APJ Community Leader Award 2024 "Think Big" (2024) # Community Leader - Security-JAWS (2016 - now) | https://s-jaws.connpass.com/ - JAWS-UG Tokyo (2018 - 2023) | https://jawsug.connpass.com/ - X-Tech JAWS (2017 - 2022) | https://xtechjaws.doorkeeper.jp/ # Community / Event Chairman - JAWS DAYS 2019 Chairman (Onsight/2,300 Participants) - JAWS SONIC 2020 / MIDNIGHT JAWS 2020 Chairman (24-hours/Domestic Virtual Online) - JAWS PANKRATION 2021 Chairman (24hours/Global Virtual Online) - JAWS PANKRATION 2024 Chairman (24hours/Global Virtual Online) # Speaker - AWS Summit Tokyo 2015 - AWS Summit Tokyo 2020 - AWS Summit Tokyo 2021 - AWS Summit Tokyo 2022 - AWS APAC Community Summit 2022 - AWS Dev Day 2022 Day0 - AWS Summit Tokyo 2023 - AWS re:Inforce 2024 - AWS Summit Tokyo 2024 - AWS Community Day Taiwan 2024 - AWS Summit Tokyo 2025 # Interesting - CNAPP - Multi-Cloud - Security Observability - Threat Intelligence - Community Management # Book - 効果的な導入・運用のための Amazon Web Services活用入門 | https://book.mynavi.jp/ec/products/detail/id=54224 - ITロードマップ 2023年版 | https://str.toyokeizai.net/books/9784492581209/ - マルチクラウドセキュリティの教科書 | https://www.shoeisha.co.jp/book/detail/9784798190853

name: Simone Merlini
bio:
Simone is CEO and CTO at beSharp, an Italian AWS Advanced Consulting Partner focused on cloud architectures and cloud-native software development. He holds a Ph.D. in Computer Science, and he serves as an adjunct professor of Cloud Computing at the University of Pavia. In 2012 he co-founded the first AWS User Group in Italy, and he's currently the organizer of the AWS User Group Milan.


Simone has been using the AWS cloud since 2007. Since then, he has successfully guided and developed many projects focusing on cloud infrastructures, continuous deployment, backup and disaster recovery, serverless, cloud security, and networking. He's also actively involved in the development of Leapp, an open-source project for managing and securing cloud access in multi-account environments.


Simone is an active member of the AWS Community. As an APN Ambassador, he participates in several events and conferences as a speaker. He's also the editor in chief and writer for Proud2beCloud, a blog by beSharp aimed to share highly specialized AWS knowledge to enable adoption of cloud technologies.

name: Slobodan Stojanović
bio: Slobodan Stojanović is the CTO and co-founder of Vacation Tracker, an application that simplifies employee leave and absence management for organizations, AWS Serverless Hero, and JS Belgrade meetup co-organizer

name: Sridevi
bio: Experienced and Certified Cloud Professional with a more than 2 decades of demonstrated history of working in the IT industry building new solutions and solving Technical challenges for multiple global clients.

name: Srushith Repakula
bio:
Srushith Repakula is the Head of Engineering at KonfHub, a one stop platform for technical conferences built on AWS stack, primarily using serverless and other associated services. His first taste of serverless was participating in the AWS Chatbot Challenge''  he fell instantly in love with the technology and the unparalleled passion for Lambdas henceforth began.


He is passionate about building serverless applications and sharing his experiences at various events across the globe, and believes serverless is the future of software development and the way applications will be built moving forward. He also thinks it will empower every developer to easily migrate to the cloud and start building with the least friction. He anticipates serverless becoming the cheapest supercomputer at the hands of every developer!


Srushith recently founded the Tirupati AWS User Group and also started the first serverless-focused meetup group in Bangalore where he presents and encourages young passionate members to get involved: there are over 6,000+ members across both groups! He also started Serverless Saturdays, a monthly webinar focusing on everything serverless and helping developers not only kick start their serverless journey, but also provide an opportunity to get access to learnings and experiences from experts across the globe.

name: Stav Ochakovski
bio: I'm Stav, a DevOps Engineer at Beacon where I manage highly scalable, multicloud environments with a focus on Kubernetes, CI/CD, and making sure logs actually tell you something useful. I'm also an AWS Containers Hero, with a strong interest in cloud-native identity and infrastructure security. I come from a background in DevOps engineering and instruction, and I try to bring that mix of hands-on experience and clear communication into everything I do. Outside of work, I'm a pastry-chef school graduate and a licensed skipper. Sessionnize profile: https://sessionize.com/stav-ochakovski/

name: Stephen Sennett
bio: Stephen is an experienced cloud technologist, working with AWS for over a decade as an architect, consultant, engineer, and educator. Stephen was a member of the AWS Community Builders program between 2021 and 2024, acting as a mentor to others in the AWS community, and serving as a public speaking coach for upcoming thought leaders through the AWS New Voices program. He is an experienced keynote speaker, delivering sessions at AWS Community Days, AWS Summits, and other tech conferences around the world. As a Content Architect with A Cloud Guru and Pluralsight, he has directly supported the teaching of hundreds of thousands of students around the world on their cloud journey. He currently works as a Lead at V2 AI in Australia, specialising in cloud strategy and architecture. Outside of his professional role, he is an active volunteer emergency management officer, and non-profit board director.

name: Steve Teo
bio: AWS Community Hero since 2020 AWS User Group Singapore Leader I specialize in Cloud Architecture and Security

name: TaeSeong Park
bio: 🤗 Open to networking 🎬 Joined AWS Community since 2017 🙋🏻 Ex AWS Community Builder 🎤 Organizer of frontend(https://github.com/public-frontend-group/meetup), gametech(https://github.com/awskrug/gametech-group), gudi(area of Seoul - https://github.com/awskrug/gudi-group) meetup in South Korea 🕹️ Current Unity 2D Developer 🤖 Building Apps with AI tools 📱 Ex React & React-Native Developer (also Node.js, serverless framework, Amplify framework) 🧑🏻‍💻 Trying to build whatever I want

name: Takuya Tachibana
bio:
Takuya Tachibana is the CEO of Heptagon inc. and Director of DigitalCube Co. Ltd. He is in the business of solving countryside issues using AWS.


Since 2012, Tachibana has been contributing to the Japan AWS User Group (JAWS-UG) for more than 10 years. He has also been a speaker at over 100 community and cloud events throughout all of Japan.


He served as the JAWS-UG representative in 2016-2017 and as Chair of the JAWS DAYS 2017. He has also spoken at overseas events, such as AWS Summit Seoul, AWS Summit Beijing, and AWS Community Day APAC.


Tachibana continues to share the appeal of AWS with the local community and in the countryside areas, focused in AWS businesses utilizing serverless, machine learning, and IoT. In addition, he also advocates for AWS and encourages builders to contribute to the greater AWS community, and believes there are no limits: you can grow no matter where you live, what you do, or how old you are.

name: Teri Radichel
bio: Teri Radichel is the founder of 2nd Sight Lab, AI assisted pentesting and security research. AWS Security Hero since 2016, Author on Amazon and blog, Former IANS & SANS faculty, SANS Difference Makers Award, 30+ years experience in security, software architecture, and software development at companies of all sizes. Security certifications: GSE + 12 more, 2 masters degrees in security engineering and software engineering, BA in business, and has a cute dog. Security, AI, and Technology research blog: https://teriradichel.substack.com Linkedin: https://linkedin.com/in/teriradichel BlueSky, X, Mastodon, Threads, Instragram: @teriradichel

name: Thorsten Höger
bio: Thorsten is a Cloud Automation Evangelist at Taimos, where he advises customers on using AWS. As a developer, he focuses on improving development processes and automating everything to build efficient deployment pipelines for customers of all sizes. As a supporter of open-source software, Thorsten maintains or contributes to several projects, mainly developer tools for AWS. He is the president of the Open Construct Foundation and maintainer of the Community-Driven CDK Construct Library. He is also a board member of the support association for the AWS community in the DACH region. Before becoming self-employed, Thorsten was a developer and CTO of Germany’s first private bank running on AWS. He and his colleagues migrated the core banking system to the AWS platform in 2013. Since then, he has frequently spoken at conferences, meetups, barcamps, and other community events.

name: Tiago Rodrigues
bio: Tiago is the Head for tecRacer.com Portugal, and a Senior Cloud Consultant, an AWS Premier Tier Services Partner and AWS Advanced Tier Training Partner, operating for over a decade with offices across Germany, Switzerland, Austria, and Portugal. With diverse experience ranging from Startups to Enterprise projects, Tiago specializes in migrations from on-premises environments to the cloud, but also in modernizing architectures or implementing serverless solutions from idea to production. Beyond his professional role, Tiago is deeply committed to knowledge sharing. He actively contributes to the AWS community, e.g. through the AWS User Group Lisbon, educational workshops, and guest lectures at universities to promote a culture of learning and exploring the potential that AWS offers. Proof of his passion for education and innovation is AWSary, an open source mobile app he developed. AWSary serves as a playful yet informative AWS dictionary that helps draw diagrams for Solution Architects and provides quick insights into AWS services. Catch Tiago sharing his insights and AWS adventures on his personal blog and at events both locally in Portugal and globally online.

name: Tom McLaughlin
bio: Software engineer who likes serverless.

name: Tomasz Dudek
bio: i like ML

name: Tomasz Lakomy
bio: Tomasz Łakomy AWS Hero | Staff Software Engineer Tomasz Łakomy is a Staff Software Engineer at Runway with over a decade of experience in frontend and cloud development. Based in Poland, he focuses on building maintainable applications using TypeScript, AWS CDK, and serverless technologies. Tomasz is a proponent of "learning in public," regularly sharing his journey to help other developers navigate the complexities of cloud infrastructure. Throughout his career at companies like Stedi and OLX, Tomasz has focused on bridging the gap between product engineering and infrastructure. As a co-founder of Cloudash, he helped develop a tool designed to make monitoring serverless applications more accessible. He advocates for using the AWS Cloud Development Kit (CDK) to treat infrastructure as a natural extension of the application code. Tomasz enjoys breaking down technical topics into practical, easy-to-digest content. His contributions to the community include: Educational Content: Creating over 200 concise lessons for egghead.io on AWS Lambda, AppSync, and the CDK. Writing: Sharing technical walkthroughs and lessons learned on tlakomy.com and the DEV Community. Speaking: Presenting at various community events and conferences to discuss the practicalities of cloud-native development.

name: Tomasz Ptak
bio: I am a boring java dev with an interest in developer experience, learning, organisation development and communities. I have joined the AWS Communities world through AWS DeepRacer and am on the lookout for opportunities to support engineers, students, communities and growth-mindset organisations

name: Toshal Khawale
bio: Seasoned Tech Leader with 22+ years in engineering leadership, specializing in AWS, Azure, and cloud-native technologies. Passionate about emerging technologies, particularly AI/ML, My expertise extends beyond the corporate sphere as AWS UG Pune Leader

name: Trista Pan
bio: Trista Pan is the CTO and Co-Founder of SphereEx, an open-source commercial SaaS start-up backed by some of the worlds top VCs.

name: Vadym Kazulkin
bio: Vadym Kazulkin is ukrainian-native AWS Serverless Hero and Head of Development at ip.labs based in Bonn. Vadym has been involved with the Java ecosystem for over 25 years. His focus and interests currently include the design and implementation of highly scalable and available applications in AWS Cloud with the special passion for Serverless, Generative AI and optimization of Java applications (also using SpringBoot, Micronaut and Quarkus frameworks) on AWS Lambda. Vadym is also the co-organizer of the Java User Group Bonn meetup, a frequent speaker at various meetups and conferences and a regular author of the articles in the different published magazines and on his online blog.

name: Vanessa Santos
bio: Driven by the belief that technology should empower growth for people, communitites and business.

name: Vicky Seno
bio: Vicky "Tanya" Seno is an AWS Container Hero, Computer Science Professor at Santa Monica College, and a involved in cloud computing education. With 20+ years of enterprise Linux and DevOps experience, she brings deep technical expertise to advancing AWS education across Southern California. As an AWS Educate Cloud Ambassador and AWS Academy Accredited Educator, Professor Seno has transformed cloud education by developing one of California's first community college Bachelor's degrees in Cloud Computing. Her AWS expertise is recognized through multiple certifications and having received the Outstanding Contribution to the AWS Ecosystem Award (Digital Revolution Awards 2020). She co-organizes AWS Cloud Day and TechUp conferences that draw 300+ attendees for hands on workshops and CTF competitions, significantly increasing student AWS certification rates. Beyond academia, Professor Seno creates AWS courses for LinkedIn Learning, including AWS Cloud Migration and Quantum Computing. Her YouTube channel (sysadmgirl) reaches 40,000+ subscribers with 100+ technical tutorials.

name: Vinicius Caridá
bio: With over 15 years of experience in IT and artificial intelligence, I hold both a Master's and a PhD in Computer Science, specializing in AI. I currently hold the position of Executive Specialist in Data and AI at Itaú Unibanco, while also teaching MBA programs in machine learning, data science, and business intelligence at FIAP and ESPM. My technical expertise spans generative AI, natural language processing, virtual assistants, chatbots, recommendation engines, control systems, and the entire MLOps lifecycle. I am honored to be recognized as an AWS Machine Learning Hero, Google Developer Expert in machine learning, and Google Champion Innovator. Additionally, I contribute as an advisor and mentor for Google Startups. I have contributed to the AI community as a Microsoft MVP in AI (2021-2022), and I currently lead AWS and Machine Learning/TensorFlow user groups in São Paulo. As Co-Chair of the LatinX in AI (LXAI) workshops at prestigious international conferences like AAAI, ICML, and NeurIPS, I remain committed to fostering diversity in AI research. As a TEDx speaker and presenter at national and international events, I am passionate about sharing my knowledge and driving discussions on AI's role in creating fairer and more innovative global solutions. My work is grounded in the belief that AI and data have the power to transform industries and improve lives.

name: Virginie Mathivet
bio: 22 ans d'expérience dans l'IA, Dr en IA. Aujourd'hui fondatrice et dirigeante d'Hemelopse, qui accompagne les entreprises dans leur roadmap stratégique autour de l'IA. Aussi conférencière et auteure

name: Vit Niennattrakul
bio:
Vit Niennattrakul finished his Ph.D. in time series data mining and is also interested in machine learning, artificial intelligence, and natural language processing. He is a passionate technopreneur who founded startup companies: Stamp (an online-to-offline service provider), Triple-E (a data collection service provider), and Inspica (a data analytics solution provider). Those startup solutions are architected on AWS. He currently runs a company called DailiTech, the first Thai AWS consulting partner that helps partners and customers migrate to the cloud and deliver many born-in-the-cloud and hybrid-cloud solutions. Also, Vit is the first Thai-certified AWS Solution Architect - Professional and has also achieved other AWS certificates later on.


Vit started using AWS in 2013, sensing that AWS was the primary building block to drive innovations in Thailand. But they needed some help to unlock the path to success. They needed places to share and exchange knowledge with AWS users in Thailand. So he started building the AWS User Group (Thailand) with the help of his passionate team and AWS staff in December 2014. He and his team regularly organize meetups and collaborate with AWS on marketing events and seminars. Also, he encourages members to share their experiences and knowledge. In 2022 the community reached 10,000 members and continues to expand.

name: Vivek Raja P S
bio:
Vivek is the VP of Product and founding member at NexStem, a leading provider of Brain Computer Interfacing solutions, acknowledged as a YourStory Tech30 startup and Nasscom Deeptech emerging startup. Recognized as the youngest AWS Machine Learning Hero, he has authored multiple research papers and recently in IIM-B and holds 2 patents in AI and IoT.


Vivek leads tech cloud communities in India such as AWS User Group Madurai, making an impact on over 5000 individuals. Proficient in multi-cloud environments, he holds a Guinness World Record. With an impressive track record of 20 hackathon wins and participation in over 60, Vivek is a sought-after technical speaker, engaging in more than 300 meet-ups and conferences. Additionally, he dedicates his time as a blogger, content creator, and hackathon mentor, inspiring individuals in the domains of Tech, AI, and Cloud.

name: Vivek V [AWS Hero]
bio: AWS AI Hero | Field CTO | Former AWS Community Builder (AI Engineering) AWS Ambassador | 16x AWS (All-Star Award, AWS Gold Jacket) | CNCF Kubestronaut (5x Kubernetes) | 5x Azure | 4x AWS Certification Subject Matter Expert (SME) | AWS re:Invent Speaker I design and build intelligent systems that help teams move faster and make better decisions. With close to three decades of experience across banking and large-scale enterprises, I’ve worked on everything from massive cloud migrations to modern AI platforms running in highly regulated environments. My focus today is on AI architecture and engineering, turning ideas into reliable systems that can reason, integrate with real workloads, and operate safely at scale. I enjoy working close to the technology, shaping patterns, mentoring engineers, and helping organizations turn complexity into something practical and usable. As an AWS AI Hero, I’m passionate about sharing what truly works in the real world, focusing on practical architectures and approaches teams can confidently build on. Now, lets go build!

name: Vlad Ionescu (he/him)
bio: Vlad Ionescu (he/him) jokingly describes himself as a “Professional mistake avoider”, which he argues is a better way of saying “Independent Consultant”. His work is devoted to getting companies to high-performing levels. Vlad swings wildly on the engineer/manager pendulum, and he is always full of energy. As an Individual Contributor, he focuses on architecting resilient applications and on growing engineers. As a Manager, he focuses on empowering people to do their best work and on building sustainable companies. In parallel to his tech career, Vlad is a foodie and very much into cocktails, whiskey, and wine — finding him at a tasting or food pairing event is a sure bet. He cherishes mental health and is an avid proponent of any effort in the area.

name: Wendy Wong
bio: Data Hero Wendy Wong is a data scientist and LinkedIn Learning Instructor. As a teacher at heart, she enjoys sharing her passion writing technical blogs on dev.to. She is both an active speaker for AWS analytics and an advocate of diversity and inclusion, presenting at a number of events: AWS User Group Malaysia, Women Who Code, AWS Summit Australia 2022, AWS BuildHers, AWS Innovate Modern Applications, and many more.

name: Wojciech Dabrowski
bio: Wojciech began his IT career in 2015, working close to the code as a backend developer. Over time, his responsibilities expanded from hands-on implementation to system design, technical leadership, consulting, and architectural decision-making, with AWS as his primary area of specialization. Throughout his career, he has worked across multiple levels of abstraction: building and operating backend systems, improving DevOps and delivery practices, leading technical teams, advising organizations, and designing cloud architectures. This breadth of experience allows him to understand how technical decisions impact systems, people, and long-term results. Although he currently works as a solutions architect, Wojciech remains hands-on and stays close to implementation, believing that effective architecture must be rooted in real operational constraints. Since 2019, he has been the leader of AWS User Group Silesia in Poland, where he organizes meetups and shares practical knowledge with the local AWS community. In June 2023, he was awarded the AWS Hero title. He focuses on pragmatic engineering, clarity of thought, and straightforward solutions to complex problems.

name: Wu Sheng
bio: Tetrate Founding Engineer & Apache SkyWalking creator & AWS Container Hero

name: Yan Cui
bio: Yan is an AWS Serverless Hero and independent consultant. Yan has run production workloads at scale on AWS since 2010 and has a track record of improving businesses with technology. He has been an architect and principal engineer in a variety of industries ranging from banking, e-commerce, streaming to mobile gaming. Yan is the author of Production-Ready Serverless and co-author of Serverless Architectures on AWS, 2nd Edition. He has delivered over 200 talks at user groups and conferences around the world and is often seen rushing through Schiphol airport to feed his cat on time.

name: Yan So
bio:
Yan So is a senior data scientist with experience in applying big data machine learning to solve complex business challenges. He currently leads the Data team at Croquis Inc. and is in charge of improving personalized experiences and the recommendation system on AWS for their customers. He began his career as a software engineer with LG Electronics for 5 years, changing his career after getting a masters of data science at the University of Auckland, New Zealand.


He is a co-founder of the Data Science Group within the AWS Korea User Group (AWSKRUG) and has hosted over 30 meetups and AI/ML hands-on labs since 2017. He regularly speaks about interesting topics such as Amazon SageMaker GroundTruth at AWS Community Day, Zigzags Data Analytics Platform at AWS Summit Seoul, and a recommendation engine on Amazon Personalize at AWS Retail & CPG Day 2019. As a ML practitioner, he is interested in not only taking challenges to define and solve ML problems, but also exploring AWS AI/ML services to use in production workloads such as Amazon Sagemaker Studio.

name: Yasunori Kirimoto
bio: Yasunori Kirimoto (dayjournal.dev) is the Co-Founder and CEO of MIERUNE (mierune.co.jp), a geospatial solutions company. He has been an AWS Hero since 2022 and brings over 20 years of experience in geospatial data and applications, specializing in GIS (Geographic Information Systems) and FOSS4G (Free and Open Source Software for Geospatial). A passionate advocate for geospatial technologies, Yasunori regularly shares insights on Amazon Location Service through blog posts and community presentations. His expertise led to a collaboration with the Amazon Location Service team, and he was invited to provide early feedback on upcoming features. He also contributed to the official AWS Blog with the article “New features and developer experience with enhanced Amazon Location Service.” He has developed several geospatial tools, including the “AWS Heroes Map (heroes.dayjournal.dev) ,” an interactive way to explore AWS Heroes worldwide. This project was featured in Werner Vogels’ final keynote slides and in the event newspaper at AWS re:Invent. He also created the “AWS re:Invent Map (reinvent.dayjournal.dev) ” to help attendees navigate event venues. Yasunori actively contributes to open-source projects on GitHub. More recently, he has contributed to projects in AWS Geospatial and AWS Amplify. Geospatial Architect｜AWS DevTools Hero｜OSGeo Charter Member｜MapLibre Voting Member Co-Founder and CEO of MIERUNE｜Community - MapLibre, Amplify, Notion

name: Yusuke Kuoka
bio: Yusuke has been an AWS Container Hero since 2018, when he was working for freee K.K., a Japanese fintech company that provides a comprehensive web-based ERP suite. He introduced both self-hosted Kubernetes on AWS and an EKS-based internal platform that serves many production workloads across several companies. He also contributed to and continues to maintain various open-source projects related to Kubernetes and AWS, including actions-runner-controller (now part of GitHub), Helmfile (still widely used), and others. He is now part of SurrealDB, which builds a scalable and embeddable multi-model database, envisioning a database that is as general as it applies to many real-world workloads, like containers have been.

name: Zainab
bio:
Zainabs leadership skills have shone since arriving in Australia. Her volunteer experience at the Edmund Rice Centre was instrumental in her transition to her new life in Perth and, deciding to give back to the Centre, Zainab started a coding class for children of culturally diverse backgrounds in 2020. Zainab facilitated CoderDojo for girls for three years, which saw more than 100 girls participate in the program. She has also presented at a WiTWA event for school students and at the Big Day In event for over 500 students, motivating them to choose IT.


Zainab is also proud to share her experiences and expertise with the local and national tech communities. She has bravely stepped up to speak at numerous technical conferences starting with the TestWest 2016 motivating pair development. In 2018 she impressed the audience at the Latency Conference with her live demo, creating a production authentication platform in less than 10 minutes.


In 2020 Zainab presented reusable serverless patterns at the AWS Community Day and co-presented Design Driven Development in data and analytics at the Latency conference in Perth.


As a full-stack developer, Senior DevOps Engineer, AWS Ambassador and regular conference presenter Zainab is proud of the value that she has delivered to her clients and her community.
